How to use math to teach first graders to share Four ways to teach sharing with math. By GreatSchools Staff "That's not fair!" As the parent of a first-grader, you probably hear a lot of arguments about sharing, whether it's how many cookies each kid gets at snack time or who gets the next turn on the swing. The good news? Teaching kids to share can be a great way to help them with math skills like counting and telling time. Here are a few ways to keep it fair and build math skills: Count it out Math lesson: Counting The problem: Kids are unhappy about having to wait for their turn. The solution: Ask the kids who are waiting to count to, say, 60 (for a minute) or 100. When they are done, it's time for the next child to have a turn. Math lesson: Telling time The problem: Same as above - kids are unhappy about waiting for a turn. The solution: Instead of counting, give kids a watch, or have them look at a clock, and announce when one minute is up (or however much time everyone's decided each child gets). Split it up Math lesson: Fractions The problem: Kids are fighting over toys, like blocks or Legos. The solution: Divide the toys into equal piles. If there are 10 blocks, divide them up into two piles of five (or have the kids divide the toys themselves). Explain that you are dividing the piles evenly. Have the kids count the toys in each pile. Math lesson: Understanding numbers The problem: Kids are arguing over who gets to go first in a game. The solution: Think of a number between 1 and 10 and ask them to guess the number. Once everyone guesses, tell them the number, and then have them figure out which of them is closest. Whoever guesses the closest number gets to go first. Amphibians are a wonderful species. Amphibian means "double life" which means they spend the first part of their life under water breathing through gills. Amphibians were the first vertebrate (animals with back bones) to leave water and come live on land. They're descendants from fish. The most common amphibians are frogs and toads. Some details about amphibians are: they breathe air through lungs and skin, they're cold blooded, they have scaleless skin, they lay their eggs in moist places and at birth do not look like their parents. Frogs and toads are similar, but not exactly the same. Frogs have small thin bodies with long smooth legs, where as toads have small fat bodies and shorter thick legs. They use these legs to crawl around and only hop when they think they're in danger. They are also scattered with large bumps called warts. But you cannot get warts by touching a toad. Most toads are about three inches long and spend most of their time on dry land. But the oak toad is one inch long, and the giant toad is almost ten inches! The skin comes in many different colors, but their eyes are usually black. These large eyes are very good at seeing on land and in the water. They also have excellent hearing so they can hear your foot steps from very far away. These toads are in danger. Farming and buildings destroy their homes and water and soil pollution make them sick. As toads die, the animals that eat them disappear too. And the insect rate is increased because the toads aren't around to eat them. Toads are predators and eat mostly insects and worms. Toads eat a lot. One toad could eat 100 flies in only ten minutes! The toad only eats its prey when it's alive and moving. They can't really see the creature if it's not moving. Most toads catch their food on the end of their long, sticky tongue. They have no teeth so they're forced to swallow their food whole. Farmers tend to like toads. They eat all those pesky insects that may be harmful to their crops. Habitat and Hibernation: Toads live almost everywhere except for the North and South poles: they enjoy warm, moist places. The Spade foot toad uses its feet to dig a burrow backwards in the soil. The toad stays in its burrow for a few weeks and only comes out if it's wet or rainy. The toad hibernates until warmer weather comes because it's cold blooded and cannot generate its own body heat. Toads don't like the sun to much because it dries out their bodies. If their body loses too much water, they die. Because of this, most toads are nocturnal. The louder a male sings the most likely he is to attract a mate. To make these sounds the toad puffs air into a huge vocal sac under his chin. The females lay up to 30000 soft, clear eggs. Most toads put their eggs in long strings at the bottom of a lake, or string them around marsh plants. But the Midwife toad carries them on its back, that way they're safe from danger. Fish and other animals like to eat toad eggs. Some eggs hatch in one day, where as others take a few weeks. When it hatches, the tadpole looks like a small fish. After a few weeks the tadpole begins to grow legs and breathe air like its parents. Later its back legs get longer and its tail shrinks. Birds and snakes like to eat toads. Because of that most toads have to be equipped with some sort of defense. Most toads use camouflage to avoid getting caught, like the Cameroon Horned toad that lives in Africa. He has a leaf like patch of yellow skin on his back. But there are other ways that toads protect themselves besides blending in with their surroundings. Some puff themselves full of air so they look bigger, others show their brightly colored bellies so they look scarier! Some use poison. The American toad has poison on its back that tastes terrible, and predators will spit it out right away. In fact, many toads are poisonous, that's why you should always wash your hands after touching one. And we have um an armoire in the living room that has the knobs on. First can you make a question mark now? Great Britain is an island in the north-west Atlantic Ocean, one of the British Isles. It is the biggest European island, off the coast of Continental Europe. To the west of Great Britain is Ireland, across the Irish Sea. Across the English Channel to the south of Great Britain is France. Across the North Sea to the east is Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, Denmark and Norway. Together with many other smaller islands, Great Britain and Ireland have the name British Isles. Wood is ground up and mixed with water and other chemicals to make a thin liquid called "paper pulp". Paper pulp can be bleached to make paper more white, and dyes can be added to make colored paper. This pulp is pressed into sheets of paper. Printing is often done on paper before the paper is cut into sheets. Newsprint paper (newspaper) comes in a huge roll, and goes through the printing process as one continuous sheet. It is cut by a machine-driven guillotine blade later. The Anahim Volcanic Belt is a nearly east-west line of volcanoes stretches from the west coast of British Columbia, Canada, just north of Vancouver Island, and reaches into the Interior Plateau near Quesnel. The farther away from the coast, the younger the volcanoes tend to be. These volcanoes are thought to have formed because of the North American continent sliding westward over the Anahim hotspot. Volcanoes in this belt include the Rainbow, Ilgachuz, and Itcha Ranges, and the Nazko Cone, which last erupted about 7200 years ago. Verbs and Tenses - an Overview Time and space are basic concepts. Events take place in space over a period of time. Time is expressed in periods or points. All sentences have a verb and all verbs are marked for time. Verbs show "time" through "tense". Tenses are formed using auxiliary verbs: am is are was were be being been do does did doing done have has had be verb and adjective Are you French? No, I am not. I am not French. I am Belgian. be verb and noun Is it pork? Yes, it is. It is pork. Do you eat seafood? No, I don't. I don't eat seafood. Is he urinating out the window? No, he is not. He is just looking. He is just looking out the window. He is not urinating out the window. Did you see that? Yes. I did. I saw it. I saw that. I saw what you saw. Were you sitting at the front of the school yesterday afternoon? No, I wasn't. I wasn't there. I wasn't sitting there. I went straight home. present perfect (simple) Have you ever eaten monkey? No, I haven't. I have never tried it. present perfect continuous How long have you been living in Australia? I have been living here for about three years. past perfect simple I had already seen him. past perfect continuous I had been working there for about a year when it happened. Future "will" simple Will you get off my foot, please? Sorry! I am terribly sorry! future "will" continuous Will you be leaving today? Not today. I will be staying another night. Future "going to" simple I am going to see him tonight. Future Continuous "going to" continuous I am going to be seeing him tonight. It is best not to make assumptions about what pupils know, so we will start with some very basic concepts. Feel free to continue if you are quite sure of these ideas! A noun is a naming word and will be either masculine or feminine in French. Masculine nouns will have "un" or "le" before them, while feminine nouns will have "une" or "la" before them. A verb is a "doing" word and it may be regular or irregular. A regular verb is a verb which follows a pattern or a set of rules, while an irregular verb is one which goes its own way and has to be learned separately. An adjective is a "describing" word. It will give you information about a noun - whether something is big, small, lovely or lousy! An adverb is also a "describing" word, but it describes verbs and tells you how something is done - whether it is done slowly, well or brilliantly (these words often end in "ly" in English). Title: How to Dye Silk: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Dyeing silk is an exciting and rewarding process that allows you to create unique, vibrant, and customized textiles for various purposes such as fashion, home decoration, or arts and crafts projects. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the entire dyeing process, providing in-depth explanations, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines along the way. By following these steps carefully, you'll be able to produce stunning results with your own hand-dyed silk pieces. Materials Needed: 1. Pure silk fabric or garments (avoid blends) 2. Fabric dyes specifically designed for protein fibers like silk 3. Nonreactive dye pot (stainless steel, enamel, glass, or plastic) 4. Measuring spoons 5. pH balance adjustors (citric acid or soda ash) 6. Thermometer 7. Rubber gloves 8. Protective eyewear 9. Salt (optional, depending on dye manufacturer recommendations) 10. Urea (optional, depending on dye manufacturer recommendations) 11. Distilled water 12. Paper towels or newspapers 1 Then I came back up with my mouth closed. Should we just talk about it or read it? Getting Pok mon to Level 100 ============================== Introduction: ------------ This guide will provide you with an in-depth explanation on how to get your beloved Pok mon to level 100. This process can be time-consuming but rewarding as a fully evolved and trained Pok mon is crucial when competing against other trainers or gym leaders. The following steps are applicable to all main series Pok mon games released on Nintendo handheld consoles such as Game Boy Advance, Nintendo DS, and Nintendo 3DS systems. ### Step 1: Catch Your Pokemon The first step in this journey begins by catching the Pok mon you wish to train. You can catch wild Pok mon using Poke Balls during battles or trade them with other players. Make sure that the chosen Pok mon has potential for growth and evolves into a powerful form if possible. ### Step 2: Train Your Pokemon ------------------------------ Once caught, start battling with your new companion. Creating labels in the Gmail app is an excellent way to organize your emails and make them easier to find. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step with clear and practical instructions. **Step 1: Open the Gmail App** First, locate the Gmail app on your mobile device and tap on its icon to open it. If you're not already signed in, enter your email address and password to sign in. **Step 2: Navigate to the Menu** Once you've opened the Gmail app, look for the three horizontal lines (also known as a hamburger menu) at the top left corner of the screen. Tap on this button to reveal the navigation drawer. **Step 3: Access Settings** In the navigation drawer, scroll down until you see the "Settings" option. Tap on it to access the settings for your Gmail account. **Step 4: Select Your Account** If you have multiple accounts set up in the Gmail app, select the account where you want to create the label. After selecting the correct account, proceed to the next step. **Step 5: Choose General Settings** On the settings page, choose "General settings." This section contains various options that allow you to customize your Gmail experience. **Step 6: Scroll Down to Labels** Scroll down the general settings page until you reach the "Labels" section. Here, you can view existing labels and add new ones. **Step 7: Add a New Label** Tap on "Create new label" to start creating a new label. A pop-up window will appear asking for the name of the label. **Step 8: Name Your Label** Enter a descriptive name for your label in the text field provided. Make sure it accurately reflects the type of emails you plan to categorize under this label. For example, if you want to create a label for work-related emails, consider naming the label "Work." Key tip: Use concise and consistent names across all your labels to maintain organization. **Step 9: Save Your New Label** After entering the name for your label, tap "Save" or the checkmark at the top right corner of the pop-up window. The new label should now be visible within the "Labels" section in the general settings. **Step 10: Apply the Label to Emails** Now that you've created your label, return to your inbox by tapping the back arrow in the upper left corner repeatedly. To apply the newly created label to an email, swipe left on any message in your inbox. You will see several options; one of which is "Label as," followed by a list of available labels. Tap on the label you just created to assign it to the selected email. Applying labels makes it easy to search for specific emails later. By consistently applying relevant labels, you'll save time when looking for important messages and keep your inbox organized. Congratulations! Title: How to Host a Dinner Party Without Using the Stove Introduction: Hosting a dinner party without using your stove might seem like a challenge, but with some creativity and advanced planning, you can create an unforgettable dining experience for your guests. This guide will walk you through the steps to host a successful dinner party using alternative cooking methods such as grilling, slow cookers, and sous vide. Step 1: Plan Your Menu Key Tip: Choose dishes that can be prepared ahead of time or cooked using non-stovetop methods. Planning your menu is the first crucial step in hosting a dinner party without using the stove. Focus on recipes that can be made using appliances like slow cookers, instant pots, air fryers, or grills. You may also consider cold dishes, salads, charcuterie boards, and desserts that don't require any heat source at all. Make sure to include various options to cater to different dietary preferences and restrictions. Step 2: Create a Timeline Key Guideline: Schedule preparation tasks according to their required cooking times and serving order. Creating a timeline ensures that everything runs smoothly during the dinner party. Start by listing out every dish and its corresponding preparation steps. Next, assign estimated times for completing these tasks based on the equipment needed and when they should be served. Be mindful of lead times for certain techniques (e.g., marinating, soaking, proofing) and allow enough time for dishes to rest before serving. Step 3: Gather Necessary Equipment Key Tip: Utilize countertop appliances and outdoor tools to compensate for the lack of stovetop usage. Is it alright with you if we leave a light on? Title: Entertain Your Feelings for Someone Accurately Introduction: Entertaining your feelings for someone can be both exciting and nerve-wracking. It's essential to approach this situation with self-awareness, respect, and honesty to ensure that you handle your emotions accurately and constructively. This wiki-style guide will provide an in-depth exploration of steps, tips, and guidelines to help you navigate this experience while fostering personal growth and maintaining healthy relationships. Step 1: Acknowledge Your Feelings Before taking any action, acknowledge and understand your feelings towards the person. Are they romantic or platonic? Consider whether these feelings are genuine or simply infatuation. Becoming aware of your emotions is crucial as it sets the foundation for accurate entertainment of your feelings. Key Tip: Journaling can be helpful in understanding and organizing your thoughts and feelings about the individual. Step 2: Evaluate Compatibility Assess if the object of your affection shares similar values, interests, goals, and lifestyle choices. While physical attraction may initially draw you toward them, compatibility plays a vital role in building lasting connections. Remember, entertaining your feelings doesn't necessarily mean pursuing a relationship; instead, focus on understanding their potential suitability as a partner. Guideline: When evaluating compatibility, consider asking yourself questions such as: do our life goals align? Can we communicate openly with one another? What shared activities could strengthen our bond? Step 3: Assess Their Interest Level Pay attention to subtle cues from the person you have affections for, like body language, eye contact, and tone of voice during conversations. These signs can indicate if they share mutual interest or not. Understanding their level of interest will inform how you proceed without causing unnecessary distress for either party. Key Tip: Avoid jumping to conclusions based solely on isolated incidents-observe patterns over time before making decisions regarding their interest levels. Step 4: Engage Authentically Develop meaningful interactions by actively listening, sharing experiences, and expressing empathy. Engaging authentically allows you to build rapport and deepen emotional bonds, ultimately providing a clearer picture of whether a relationship would be feasible. Guideline: During conversation, show genuine curiosity about their opinions, aspirations, and concerns. Reflective statements, open-ended questions, and active listening demonstrate engagement and foster stronger connections. Step 5: Manage Expectations Maintain realistic expectations throughout this process. Keep in mind that even when two people share strong feelings for one another, external factors might hinder the possibility of a successful partnership. Mentally preparing for various outcomes ensures resilience and adaptability should things not progress as planned. Key Tip: Practice gratitude for the opportunity to explore these feelings, regardless of the end result. Focusing on appreciation rather than expectation creates space for positive growth. Step 6: Seek Guidance Carefully Consider seeking advice from trusted friends or family members who know both parties involved. However, exercise caution when disclosing sensitive information, ensuring confidentiality and avoiding gossip which could negatively impact relationships. Guideline: Before discussing your feelings with others, reflect on what specific guidance you seek. Articulating your needs clearly increases the likelihood of receiving valuable input. Conclusion: Entertaining your feelings for someone involves introspection, observation, authentic interaction, and careful consideration. By following these steps, tips, and guidelines, you'll be well equipped to manage your emotions responsibly, cultivate meaningful connections, and promote overall wellbeing. [8.3 sec; S throws it again, it bounces] Title: How to Change Your PS5 Background Introduction: The release of the PlayStation 5 has brought excitement to gamers worldwide with its impressive features and sleek design. Among these features is the ability to customize your home screen background. This tutorial will guide you through changing your PS5's background image using simple steps that ensure clarity and ease of application. **Step 1:** Navigate to the Home Screen ---------------------------------------- Press the PS button located at the center of your DualSense controller to access the Control Center, then select "Home" from the options presented. The Home Screen displays all installed applications (games and media) vertically down the left side of the screen while showing essential information about recent activities or updates on the right side. Why this step matters: Before changing your background, you need to be aware of where you are starting from - i.e., the default Home Screen. It also serves as a reference point throughout the process so you can see the changes made after setting a new wallpaper. **Step 2:** Enter the Settings Menu --------------------------------- From the Home Screen, navigate towards the top right corner until you find a gear icon labeled 'Settings.' Press X to open the menu. Key Tip: Familiarizing yourself with the location of different settings within the interface makes navigating future tasks more efficient. **Step 3:** Select 'Display & Video' ---------------------------------- Within the Settings menu, scroll down until you reach the "Display & Video" option. Highlight it by positioning your cursor over the text and press X to enter the submenu. Guideline: Remember that some menus may vary slightly depending on system updates; however, major categories like Display & Video remain consistent across versions. **Step 4:** Choose 'Theme' ----------------------- Under the Display & Video heading, select 'Theme'. Here you have two preinstalled themes - one dark and one light. But we want to change our background, which requires an additional step. **Step 5:** Personalize Your PS5 Experience ------------------------------------------ After selecting 'Theme', look further down the list for 'Presets,' highlight it, and click R2 to expand the selection. Now choose 'Custom' to proceed. At this stage, instead of being limited to preset themes, you now have the freedom to set your preferred background image. **Step 6:** Upload Your Chosen Image ----------------------------------- Now comes the exciting part! You get to upload your chosen image. To do this, connect your USB storage device containing the desired image file(s) to the PS5 via any available USB port. Then follow these substeps: * **Substep A:** Once connected, return to the Custom Theme section under Presets. * **Substep B:** Scroll down to 'Select Image' and hit X. * **Substep C:** Using the folder tree displayed, locate your USB drive and select the image file you wish to use. Keyboard shortcuts won't work here; therefore, manual navigation might take longer than usual. Patience is key. **Step 7:** Apply Changes ---------------------- Finally, having successfully selected your personalized image, confirm your choice by clicking 'Apply'. Congratulations! You should now enjoy your newly decorated PS5 Home Screen. Conclusion: By following these straightforward yet thorough instructions, users can effortlessly modify their PS5 background according to individual preferences. As always, remember to explore other settings too, since familiarization leads to mastery. Happy gaming! Do you want to write to your father? No for de boys and de mother. [MOT is reaching over to help Chi remove objects from the base] = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Peters/010424b.cha = = = Title: How to Change the System Language on a Mac **Introduction:** Changing the system language on your Mac can be helpful if you prefer using your computer in a different language or if you are setting up a Mac for someone who speaks another language. This tutorial will guide you through changing the system language on your Mac step-by-step with clear explanations along the way. **Step 1: Access System Preferences** Click the Apple icon (located at the top left corner) and select 'System Preferences' from the dropdown menu. The System Preferences window allows users to customize various settings related to their Mac's functionality and appearance. **Step 2: Navigate to Language & Region Settings** In the System Preferences window, click 'Language & Region.' It is usually located towards the bottom row of icons but may vary depending upon your screen resolution and size. **Step 3: Select the General Tab** Once inside the Language & Region settings, ensure that the 'General' tab is selected as this is where we make changes to our preferred language. **Key Tip**: If you cannot find these options, ensure that you have updated your macOS to its latest version because some older versions might not provide all these features. **Step 4: Choose Your Preferred Language** Under the heading 'Preferred languages', you will see a list of available languages. Scroll down until you find your desired language, then click and drag it to move it to the top of the list. The first language listed becomes your primary interface language; other applications will follow suit based on their support for translated interfaces. **Guideline**: Changing the order of the languages here does more than just change the system language; it also determines which language takes precedence when multiple languages are supported by an application. For example, if both English and Spanish are installed, choosing English first would mean most apps display menus, buttons etc., in English even if they support Spanish translation too. **Step 5: Restart Applications for Changes to Take Effect** After setting a new primary language, not all applications will immediately switch over. Some programs require a restart before reflecting the new language settings. Therefore, close any open applications and reopen them after completing these steps. **Additional Steps for Specific Versions** For macOS Mojave or earlier: - After moving your chosen language to the top of the list, log out and log back into your user account for the changes to take full effect. For macOS Catalina and later: - You don't need to log out anymore since the changes should apply instantly without needing a restart. However, keep in mind that certain third-party software may still necessitate manual intervention. **Conclusion:** Congratulations! By following this detailed tutorial, you now know how to successfully change the system language on a Mac. Remember, though, that while many applications adapt well to language changes, some might retain elements in the original language due to limited localization efforts. yeah, then I go in the Sarah. Calculating the rate of temperature increase is an important skill that can be used in various fields such as physics, chemistry, engineering, and environmental science. This tutorial will guide you through the process of calculating the rate of temperature increase using real-world data. **Step 1: Gather Data** The first step in calculating the rate of temperature increase is to gather temperature data over time. You may collect this data yourself using thermometers or other temperature measuring devices, or obtain it from existing sources like weather reports or scientific studies. Make sure that your data points are evenly spaced in time (e.g., every minute, hour, day). A table format would be ideal for organizing these sets of data. Key Tip: For accurate results, ensure that the units of measurement for both time and temperature remain consistent throughout your dataset. **Step 2: Plot Your Data** Once you have collected your data, plot the temperatures against time on a graph. This visual representation allows us to observe trends more clearly. The x-axis represents time while the y-axis stands for temperature. Each point corresponds to one set of your recorded data where the position along the x-axis indicates when the measurement was taken and the height above the axis shows the observed temperature at that moment. Guideline: Use graphing software or tools if available but don't worry if they aren't; a simple hand-drawn chart can also work effectively. **Step 3: Identify the Trend** Look at your plotted data and identify whether there is an overall upward trend suggesting a rise in temperature. If the dots generally move from bottom left to top right, then you likely have a positive slope which means a rising temperature trend. Key Tip: Disregard short term fluctuations and focus on longer-term patterns. These could result from random errors or natural variations within the system being measured. **Step 4: Choose Two Points** Choose two points on your line - one near the beginning and one towards the end - to calculate the change in temperature between them. Record their corresponding times and temperatures accurately. Guideline: Selecting points close together might limit variation, whereas choosing ones too far apart may introduce unnecessary complexity due to potential changes in conditions during that period. **Step 5: Calculate the Change in Time & Temperature** Subtract the earlier time from the later time to get the change in time. Similarly, subtract the initial temperature from the final temperature to find out how much the temperature has increased. Mathematically speaking: T = TF -TI t = Tfinal - Tinitial Where: T is the change in temperature, TF is the final temperature, TI is the initial temperature, t is the change in time. **Step 6: Compute the Rate of Temperature Increase** Finally, divide the change in temperature by the change in time to determine the average rate of temperature increase over the duration chosen. Rate = T / t This gives you the average rate of temperature increase per unit time. Remember, though, that rates calculated this way only represent averages across the specified interval. They do not necessarily reflect constant increases nor predict future changes outside of that range. By following these steps meticulously, you should now be able to calculate the rate of temperature increase with confidence! It may not look sticky, but water molecules love to stick together. Water molecules pull and tug on each other from all directions. Look at a glass of water. A special stretchy "skin" forms at the top of a glass of water because the molecules are pulling from the sides and from below, but not from the top. This "skin" is known as surface tension. Surface tension allows the water level to get higher than the walls of the glass without spilling! Let's put this to the test. 1. Fill a glass to the top with tap water. 2. Set your glass on a paper towel. 3. Carefully add pennies one at a time. Be sure to keep count. 4. Observe the water at the top of the cup. Eventually it will begin to bulge out. 5. Add pennies until the water begins to drip over the edge of the glass. How many pennies were you able to get into the cup? 6. Now, remove all of the pennies and get a new cup of tap water. This time add several squirts of dishwashing liquid to the cup and try the activity again. 7. Were you able to add more pennies or did it hold less? What do you think is happening? Title: How to Browse HEIC Photos on Windows HEIC (High Efficiency Image Container) is a file format developed by Apple as an alternative to JPEG for storing photos. While this format offers many benefits such as smaller file size without compromising image quality, compatibility issues arise when you try to open them on non-Apple devices like Windows computers. However, there are several ways around this limitation which we will discuss in detail below. **Method 1: Using a Third-Party Viewer** This method involves downloading and installing a third-party application that can read and display HEIC files on your Windows PC. Here's how: Step 1: **Choose a Compatible Application** There are numerous free and paid applications available online that support viewing of HEIC files. Some popular options include CopyTrans HEIC, HEIF Image Utility, and HEVC Video Extensions from Device Manufacturer. Research each option thoroughly before deciding which one suits your needs best. Key Tip: Always ensure you download software from trusted sources to avoid malware or viruses. Step 2: **Download the Software** Once you've chosen your preferred software, visit its official website and download the setup file. Save it to a location easy to find later, such as your desktop. Step 3: **Install the Software** Double click the setup file to start installation. Follow the prompts to complete the process. This usually includes accepting terms and conditions, choosing a destination folder, and configuring settings if necessary. Step 4: **Restart Your Computer** After successful installation, restart your computer so changes take effect. Step 5: **Open the File** Now you should be able to double click any .heic file and have it open with your newly installed viewer. **Method 2: Converting HEIC Files to Another Format** If you don't want to install additional software just to view these images, another solution is converting them into a more universally accepted format like JPG or PNG. Step 1: **Find an Online Converter** Numerous websites offer free services to convert HEIC files. Websites like iLoveIMG, CloudConvert, and Zamzar provide this service. Again, make sure to choose reputable sites to protect your data. Step 2: **Upload the File** Follow the site's instructions to upload the HEIC photo you wish to convert. Typically, this requires browsing your folders and selecting the file. Step 3: **Select Output Format** Choose either JPG or PNG as your output format depending on what works best for you. Step 4: **Start Conversion** Initiate the conversion process. Depending on the size of the file, this could take anywhere from seconds to minutes. Step 5: **Download Converted File** After completion, download the converted file back onto your device. Now you can view it using any standard image viewer on Windows. Guidelines: Remember, while convenient, automatic online conversions may pose privacy risks since they require uploading potentially sensitive images to external servers. Only convert images whose content you're comfortable sharing publicly. In conclusion, although Microsoft Windows doesn't natively support HEIC formats, users aren't left stranded thanks to various workarounds explained above. Whether through installing dedicated viewers or converting images, enjoying your HEIC photos on a Windows machine is entirely possible. yeah, but you went before it got broken last night, huh? In the table, the elements are placed in the order of their atomic numbers starting with the lowest number of one, hydrogen. The atomic number of an element is the same as the number of protons in that particular nucleus of an atom. In the periodic table the elements are arranged into "periods" and "groups." A row of elements across the table is called a "period". Each period has a number; from 1 to 8. Period 1 has only 2 elements in it: hydrogen and helium. Period 2 and Period 3 both have 8 elements. Other periods are longer. Elements in a period have consecutive atomic numbers. One day, a boy named Tim had a troubled face. He wanted to sell his toys, but he didn't know how. Adding prepaid funds to your Apple Pay Cash card on your iPhone is a convenient way to have money readily available for purchases, payments, or transfers using Apple Pay. This tutorial will provide you with an in-depth guide on how to add prepaid funds to your Apple Pay Cash card. **Step 1: Open the Wallet app** The first step is to open the preinstalled Wallet application on your iPhone. The Wallet app stores all your credit cards, debit cards, rewards cards, tickets, and other passes that are compatible with Apple Pay. It also houses your Apple Pay Cash card, which functions similarly to a digital debit card. **Step 2: Tap on your Apple Pay Cash card** Once the Wallet app is opened, locate your Apple Pay Cash card among the list of items stored within. If you haven't yet set up your Apple Pay Cash card, refer to this [Apple Support article](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT208534) before proceeding. After finding the Apple Pay Cash card, tap on its image to access further options. **Step 3: Select '...' (three dots)** At the top right corner of your Apple Pay Cash card screen, there should be three small dots arranged vertically - known as an ellipsis. Tapping on these dots reveals additional actions related to managing your Apple Pay Cash card. **Step 4: Choose 'Add Money'"** From the menu presented after tapping the ellipsis, select 'Add Money.' Choosing this option initiates the process of adding prepaid funds to your Apple Pay Cash card. Note that if someone sends you money via Messages or another method supported by Apple Pay Cash, those amounts do not count towards your balance limit but rather serve as transactional float until used or transferred out. **Step 5: Enter amount** You will now see a prompt asking you to enter the amount you wish to add to your Apple Pay Cash card. Type in the desired value using the number pad provided. Keep in mind that minimum and maximum limits exist; currently, they stand at $10 and $3,000 per transaction respectively. Also, ensure you only input whole dollar figures since cents aren't accepted when adding funds manually. **Step 6: Confirm payment source** After entering the desired amount, you need to choose from where the funds will originate. Ideally, link a bank account directly to avoid fees associated with debit or credit cards. However, if none is linked already, you can temporarily opt for a debit or credit card, understanding that a fee may apply. Once selected, hit 'Next'. **Step 7: Authenticate payment method** For security purposes, authenticate your chosen payment source either through Touch ID, Face ID, or passcode depending on what your device supports. By doing so, you confirm ownership and authorize the transfer of funds from said source into your Apple Pay Cash card. **Step 8: Review information & complete transaction** Lastly, review all entered details one final time before hitting 'Add' to conclude the transaction. Please note that processing times might vary based on factors like weekends or holidays. Generally speaking, though, transactions initiated during business hours typically reflect instantly. _Key Tips:_ * Always double-check entered values to prevent errors or unnecessary delays due to incorrect inputs. * Link a bank account directly to enjoy fee-free transactions while loading your Apple Pay Cash card. * Remember that manual fund addition requires whole dollar figures without decimal points or cents. In addition, the Japanese altered or destroyed various Korean monuments including Gyeongbok Palace and documents which portrayed the Japanese in a negative light were revised. The most-known work of Kant is the book "Critique of Pure Reason" ("Kritik der reinen Vernunft") that Kant published in 1781. Kant called his way of thought "critique", not philosophy. Kant said that critique was a preparation for establishment of real philosophy. According to Kant, people should know what human reason can do and which limits it has. In the "Critique of Pure Reason" Kant wrote several limits of human reason, to both feeling and thinking something. For sensation, there are two limits inside of human perception: space and time. There are no physical objects, but the limitations of our mind that work whenever we feel something through our senses. For thinking, he said there are twelve categories or pure rational concepts, divided into four fields: quantity, quality, relation and modality. Kant thought human reason applied those ideas to everything. Once upon a time, there was a gifted little girl. She could do many things that other kids could not do. Read the full transcript Learn about Measuring Segments In this lesson, let's learn how to measure segments. So what we will learn is how do we measure line segments. And the way we do that before we get to the two problems given to us, if we have a line segment AB, it starts here and it ends here, the measurement of this line segment, the length of the measurement of this can be simply computed by looking at the absolute value of A minus B, which is the value of A on the number line and the value of B on the number line. Let's actually use capital letters for both. So we can calculate the absolute value, or it could be B minus Al; it does not make a difference. Since we are computing the absolute value anyway, A minus B and B minus A will remain the same. Let's actually use a couple of problems, so let's try this. We need to find the length of the segment DC, so that's D and C. So what have we learned? What we have to do is find the absolute value of D minus C or absolute value of C minus D. What is D minus C? Well, D is at 4.5 and C is 1, so 4.5 - 1 = 3.5. Absolute value of 3.5 equals 3.5. Let's do it this way, C minus D. What is C? C is 1 minus-what is D? 4.5. 1 - 4.5 = -3.5, absolute value of -3.5 is 3.5. So what's the length of this segment? 3.5. All we did was take the two points, plug in their values and subtract them and make sure we take only the positive. Whatever the number is, take the positive value of it. Let's do it again for a different problem. In this case, we have to measure the length of the segment XY and XZ. Let's do XY first. So X minus Y, absolute value is what we need. What is X? X is 1. What is Y? Y is 6. So 1 - 6 = -5, absolute value of that is 5. So that's the length of XY. Length of XZ, which is this one, is X minus Z. What is X? X is still 1. What is Z? Z is -4. What is 1 + 4? It equals 5, minus and minus, subtraction of a negative number is the same as adding the number. 1 + 4 = 5. What's the absolute value of 5? 5. So length of XY is equal to length of XZ. These are called congruent lines because they are exactly the same size. And the way you represent two lines of the same size is you put ticks in them, so XY, XZ, if we put a single tick mark, a single check mark, these means these lines are of the same size or congruent. So yes, are these segments congruent? Yes, the two segments XY and XZ are indeed congruent. This isn't Earl that's a little boy. The city has international schools that teach foreign children. Title: How to Become a Go-To Person for Answers and Solutions Introduction: In both personal and professional settings, go-to people are individuals who possess extensive knowledge, strong problem-solving skills, and excellent communication abilities. They're reliable resources whom others trust to provide accurate information or help resolve issues effectively. This wikiHow will guide you through the process of becoming a go-to person by following these steps. Step 1: Develop Strong Communication Skills Effective verbal and written communication is crucial when trying to become a valuable resource for answers and solutions. Here are some ways to improve your communication skills: * Practice active listening - pay attention to what others say without interrupting them, ask clarifying questions if needed, and summarize their points to ensure understanding. * Improve vocabulary and grammar - read books, newspapers, and articles regularly to expand your vocabulary. Use online tools like Grammarly to enhance writing accuracy. * Learn nonverbal cues - understand body language, facial expressions, and gestures to interpret emotions better and respond appropriately during conversations. Key tip: Be concise but thorough in your responses; avoid unnecessary jargon that might confuse listeners or readers. Guideline: Regularly engage in meaningful conversations with diverse groups of people to sharpen your communication abilities. Step 2: Broaden Your Knowledge Base Aim to acquire new skills and learn about various topics by following these strategies: * Read widely - consume content from different genres (fiction, non-fiction), subjects (science, history, technology), and formats (books, blogs, podcasts). * Attend workshops, seminars, and conferences related to your field or areas of interest. * Engage in lifelong learning - pursue additional education, certifications, or training opportunities throughout your career. Key tip: Focus on gaining foundational knowledge across multiple disciplines rather than specializing too narrowly. Guideline: Diversify your sources of information to minimize bias and gain well-rounded perspectives. Another part of metallurgy is making parts from metals. These parts must be made so they will not break when they are used. Metallurgists look to make metal that meets the needs of the process it will be used in. Sometimes the metal must be lightweight or low in density. Other times it must be tough (not easily broken). The metallurgist must follow directions when making the part to know what metal to use. the process of metal selection is known as the materials selection process. Metallurgists use mathematical equations to determine what are the most important physical features of the metal. Common desired characteristics include ductility, toughness, corrosion resistance, and strength. Different metals and their associated features are often graphed on Ashby plots. Uhhh, I'm Confused! must learn how to identify letters with their vocal gestures and sounds before being able to become readers (phonemic awareness). Short vowels are very hard for students to learn and are arguably some of the most important correspondences for children to master. They appear often in their readings and must be mastered before long vowels and irregular correspondences are taught. By learning that short U says /u/, (u=/u/), students will be able to read short U words with more accuracy and those words will eventually become part of their sight vocabulary. Students will learn how to identify the short U sound in words by learning a visual and oral representation of the sound, practicing printing the letter U, identifying the short U sound in spoken words provided by the teacher after a word is modeled for them and will practice how to identify the short U sound in written words by reading a book together with a partner (each reading every other page), and will practice finding the /u/ sound by completing a picture worksheet in which they circle the pictures that represent words with the short Enlarged picture of a person looking confused and saying 'Uhhhh' and 'U says /u/' on white butcher paper on the board, a poster taped to board with the tongue twister, 'Uncle was upset when he was unable to put his umbrella up' , primary paper and pencil for each student, dry erase board and markers for teacher to print u on board for modeling, a baggie for each student with letterboxes and the letters they will need (c, d, g, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, and u).We will complete the following words in our class LBL: sun, cup, jug, pup, jump, drum, truck, and plum, the book students will read in pairs, Bud the Sub, and a picture worksheet in which students identify which of the following words have the /u/ sound in them: bus, six, umbrella, music, cup, hat, jump, pumpkin, sun, four, lollipop, plane, butterfly, one-hundred, fan, unicycle, tooth, frog, truck, stapler, jellyfish, drum, fish, and 1) First, I will explain to the students that words are made up of letters. These letters create different sounds and when put together, create words. Learning the sound that each letter stands for is important because they help us to become good readers. I will explain, "One sound that can be found in words is /u/. The sound /u/ is made by the letter U when it is a short vowel. Today we will be learning about the letter U and how to recognize words in which the letter U makes the /u/ sound (u=/u/). After we can recognize U as /u/ in spoken words, we will learn how to read u=/u/ in written word through a book." 2) Next, I will say to students, "We can hear the /u/ sound in lots of words. One way I remember the /u/ sound is when I get confused. See the man in this picture? His name is Hugo. He is confused while he is reading the paper and says "Uhh". Can everyone say that with me? (Wait for students to do it with me,) "Uhh". Good! Sometimes when I get confused or don't know the answer to a question, I say "Uhh..." Has that every happened to any of you? (Wait for student response). Well when that happens to me, I often put my finger to my head like this (show them finger on temple) and say "Uhh". Can you do that with me? (Practice saying 'uh' and putting our finger on our temple) Very good! Now every time we hear /u/ in a word today I want us all to put our finger on our head and say "Uhh". This will help us remember what U sounds like as a short vowel. If you need help remembering the sound the letter U makes, look at Hugo on the board and he will remind you." 3) I will say to students, "Now, we are going to say the tongue twister on this poster. (I will point to the poster taped on the board.) Every time you hear /u/ I want you to put your finger on your temple and say "Uhh" like we practiced, ok? I'll say it once first and then we will say it together adding the /u/'s. The tongue twister is 'Uncle was upset when he was unable to hang the umbrella up.' Now let's do it together: Uncle (Uhh) was upset (Uhh) when he was unable (Uhh) to put (Uhh) his umbrella (Uhh) up (Uhh). Very good! 4) I will say to students, "We found the /u/ sound in those words because it was the first sound in the words. For example, we knew upset had the /u/ sound in it because the first sound in the word was /u/. Now lets try to find /u/ in words where it is not the first sound. I'll show you how I find it in a word and then you will get to try one too. I will see if I can find /u/ in the word hundred by saying it very slowly and listening for the confused /u/ sound." (I will write and how I sound it out on the dry erase board with dry erase markers.) "Hundred. Hhh-uuu-nn-d-r-ed. H-uuuuuuu-nd-red There it was! H-uuu, I hear the confused /u/ sound right after the 'h' in the word hundred. Now let's try a few together. I'm going to tell you two words and you will raise your hand and tell me which word has the confused /u/ sound. Can you hear /u/ in Walk or Run? Ugly or Pretty? Gross or Yucky? Car or Truck? Bus or Van? Caterpillar or Butterfly? Pumpkin or Gourd? Skip or Jump? Sucker or Lollipop? Fudge or Chocolate? 5) I will say to students, "Now that we know how to spot the sound /u/ in words, lets review how we recognize the letter U in words. Everyone needs to get out one sheet of primary paper and a pencil. I am going to show you how I write it on the board. Then I want you to try it. I make a lowercase u by starting at the fence and going down to the sidewalk, curving back up to the fence, and going straight back down to the sidewalk (printing this as I say it on the board). So, we go down the to ditch from the sidewalk, curve up, and go straight back down (drawing it again.) Now I want you all to print your u's this way 10 times. I will walk around and help you." 6) I will then hand out a baggie to each student for our class LBL. I will say to the students: "We are going to try to arrange our words by their sounds and by the way our mouth moves and then we will be able to see the /u/ sound in the word by itself. For example (writing on board), bug. Bbbbbuuuuuuggggggg. B-u-g. I hear three sounds in that word. (Draw 3 letterboxes on the board and draw each letter as I say it and make the mouth moves in its box). B-, that's b. B-u-, then a u. B-u-g, and lastly, a g. Now let's do some together. For this word, make sure you have 3 boxes. Now everyone try the word sun." I will walk around and see that everyone is getting it and clarifying if needed. I will continue this way for the rest of the words and tell them the number of boxes needed each time. After the students have spelled all the words, they will be given a list of the same words and asked to read them. 7) I will then place the students in pairs and give them each a copy of the book, Bud the Sub, which focuses on short u words. I will give the students a brief booktalk before placing them into pairs: "Today, you will be reading Bud the Sub with your partners. Each of you will read every other page. I will walk around and see if you need help. This book is about a sub named Bud and his boss Gus. Gus gets in Bud the Sub and they head out to sea. But, all of a sudden, Bud sees a tugboat that has crashed. Do you think Bud and Gus will help the people in the tugboat? Do you think the boat might sink? We will have to read to find 8) After reading the book in pairs with peer and teacher scaffolding as needed, I will ask the students to return to their desks. I will hand out a picture worksheet from enchanted learning. This worksheet has picture representations of the words bus, six, umbrella, music, cup, hat, jump, pumpkin, sun, four, lollipop, plane, butterfly, hundred, fan, unicycle, tooth, frog, truck, stapler, jellyfish, drum, fish, and numbers. We will decide as a class what word the pictures represent so that there is no confusion. The students will complete the worksheet individually and without help. 9) I will assess students on their individual picture worksheet because this is the only activity they did without any help. This will show their ability to detect the short u sound (/u/) in words and will show me if they are ready to move on to a new correspondence or if they need more work with u=/u/. 1) Enchanted Learning, Short U Theme Page, 2) Cushman, Sheila. Title: How to Endorse a Check Properly Endorsing a check is an essential skill that ensures the smooth transfer of funds from the payer to the payee. This tutorial will provide you with a comprehensive guide on endorsing a check properly, including key tips and guidelines. By following these steps, you'll be able to endorse checks correctly and confidently. ### Step 1: Understand What Endorsing a Check Means An endorsement is a legal signature placed on the back of a check by the payee (the person or entity to whom the money is owed) or their authorized representative. The purpose of this endorsement is to authorize the bank to negotiate or deposit the check into the appropriate account. If the endorsement is missing, illegible, or improper, the bank may refuse to process the check, causing delays and potential financial loss. Therefore, learning how to endorse a check properly is crucial. ### Step 2: Gather Necessary Information Before endorsing a check, ensure you have all necessary information at hand. You need access to your checking account number and know which account type you want to deposit the check into - savings, checking, etc. Having this information ready saves time when depositing the check at a branch or via mobile banking. ### Step 3: Identify the Payee Line Locate the "Pay to the Order Of" line on the front side of the check. Verify that your name, as written on the account, matches the payee listed here. Make any required adjustments if there are spelling errors or outdated names associated with the account. ### Step 4: Prepare the Backside of the Check Flip over the check so its back faces upwards. Find the blank space located above the words "Endorse Here." This section serves as the area where you should place your endorsement. ### Step 5: Sign Your Name Exactly As It Appears On Your Account Sign your full name exactly as it appears on your checking account. Consistency between signatures is critical; otherwise, banks might reject the check due to discrepancies. Use black or blue ink only, as other colors can cause scanning issues during processing. Key Tip: Always double-check your signature before submitting the check for deposit. An incorrect endorsement could lead to rejection or delay. ### Step 6: Add Additional Instructions (Optional) If needed, include additional restrictions or limitations regarding the handling of the check below your signed endorsement. Some common examples include restrictive endorsements like: * "For Deposit Only": Allows depositing the check but prevents withdrawals using the paper instrument itself. Ideal for mobile deposits. * "Account Number XXXXXXXXXX": Specifies the exact account number where the funds must be credited upon clearing. Helpful for multiple accounts under one name. Avoid adding unnecessary details or alterations, as they increase the likelihood of errors or fraudulent activities. ### Step 7: Complete the Deposit Process After successfully endorsing the check, proceed with the intended method of deposit - either through a physical branch, ATM, or mobile app. Remember to retain copies or photographs of both sides of the endorsed check until confirmation of successful clearance reaches your account. By carefully following these steps, you're now equipped with the knowledge to endorse checks securely and efficiently. Applying these techniques guarantees seamless transactions while minimizing risks and uncertainties involved in the negotiation of paper instruments. Happen cow watching Rusty down 'n 'ere. Physical fitness refers to good body health. It is dependent on genetic determinators and also on social, economic and ecological factors. That means, one's genes are partly responsible for one's physical health, but also other circumstances: where you live, how clean or polluted your water and the air around you is and also how good your social and medical system is. It is also the result of regular exercise, proper diet and nutrition, and proper rest for physical recovery. A person who is physically fit will be able to walk or run without getting breathless and they will be able to carry out the activities of everyday living and not need help. How much each person can do will depend on their age and whether they are a man or woman. A physically fit person usually has a normal weight for their height. The relation between their height and weight is called their Body Mass Index. A taller person can be heavier and still be fit. If a person is too heavy or too thin for their height it may affect their health. Better health is central to human happiness and well-being. It also makes an important contribution to economic progress, as healthy populations live longer, are more productive, and save more. Many factors influence health status and a country's ability to provide quality health services for its people. " What did they height erupt for? " THE JACKDAW AND THE EAGLE. An Eagle flew down from the top of a high rock, and settled upon the back of a lamb, and then, instantly flying up into the air again, bore his bleating prize aloft in his talons. A Jackdaw, who sat upon an elm, and beheld his exploit, resolved to imitate it. So, flying upon the back of a ram, and entangling his claws in the wool, he fell a-chattering and attempting to fly; by which means he drew the observation of the shepherd upon him, who, finding his feet hampered in the fleece of the ram, easily took him, and gave him to his boys for their sport and diversion, saying, "The silly bird thought he was an Eagle; but, no doubt, by this time he has found out he is but a Jackdaw. Title: How to Enable Diction on an iPhone Dictation is a convenient feature on iPhones that allows users to speak commands or enter text instead of typing. This can be especially helpful when you're multitasking, have mobility issues, or simply prefer speaking to typing. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to enable dictation on your iPhone. **Step 1: Check Compatibility** Before proceeding, ensure your device supports dictation. The feature is available on all iPhones from the 4S model onwards running iOS 5 or later versions. If your device meets these criteria, continue to the next step. **Step 2: Access Settings App** On your home screen, find the gray app with gears labeled "Settings." Tap it once to open. This will take you to the main settings menu where various customizable features are housed. **Step 3: Navigate to General Settings** Scroll down until you see the section titled "General." It has a grey background and a gear icon. Tapping here gives access to more specific settings related to general phone functionality. **Step 4: Select Keyboard** Under the 'General' options, select 'Keyboard.' This option controls all aspects of keyboard behavior including auto-correction, predictive text, and most importantly for our purpose - dictation. **Step 5: Activate Dictation Feature** Look for the "Enable Dictation" switch at the top of this page. When activated (switched right), your device will download necessary language files over Wi-Fi. Be aware that turning on dictation may impact battery life due to additional data usage. Wait patiently as the download progresses; do not interrupt the process. **Key Tips:** * Make sure your microphone isn't covered or obstructed by anything while using dictation. Obstructions could interfere with accurate voice recognition. * Speak clearly and slowly during dictation to improve accuracy. Pauses between sentences also help. * Although internet connection isn't required every time you use dictation after initial setup, having stable connectivity enhances performance. **Step 6: Test Your Microphone** To confirm everything works correctly, try dictating a short sentence. Press the microphone button located near the space bar whenever the keyboard appears. Once done talking, wait for the transcription to appear. Correct any errors if needed. **Guidelines:** * Practice regular maintenance checks to keep your software up-to-date. Regular updates often include bug fixes and improvements for built-in features like dictation. * Remember, dictation might not perfectly transcribe complex phrases, names, or technical terms. Always proofread before sending important messages or documents. Congratulations! You've successfully enabled dictation on your iPhone. With practice, this tool can significantly streamline communication and productivity tasks. Happy dictating! Weather happens because different parts of the Earth get different amounts of heat from the Sun. This makes different climates. The tropics get the most heat because the Sun shines straight down on them, while the poles get the least heat because the Sun shines on them from a low angle. Warmer air is lighter than cooler air and rises higher in the sky by convection. Located within the central part of the lowlands is the "Central Belt", a rectangle of land roughly 88 kilometres (55 miles) from West to East and 48 kilometres (30 miles) North to South. About half of the population of Scotland lives within these roughly 4,530 square kilometres (or 1,750 square miles), which is a little more than 2% of the total land area of Scotland. This is the area between Scotland's two largest cities - Glasgow, at the Central Belt's Western end, and Edinburgh, at the Central Belt's Eastern end. This area is geographically bound by two bays of water - the Firth of Clyde to the West and the Firth of Forth to the East. It is the most fertile Earth in Scotland, which is why it is so population-dense, compared to the rest of the country. There they still were--the shuttered and overgrown windows. One way players can enhance their experience with this game is by linking their **Twitch** account to receive special rewards through **Twitch Drops**. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to get these drops: ### Step 1: Create a Twitch Account If you don't already have a Twitch account, create one at <https://www.twitch.tv/>. This will allow you to watch streams, chat with other viewers, and most importantly, receive Twitch Drops. Having a verified email address associated with your Twitch account increases security and ensures smooth delivery of any potential drops. ### Step 2: Link Your Lost Ark and Twitch Accounts Navigate to the official Lost Ark website (<https://la.plaync.com/>) and log into your account. Once logged in, look for the "Link Twitch" button usually found under the "Account" or "Settings" section. Clicking this button will redirect you to Twitch's website where you'll need to authorize the connection between the two accounts. Make sure you are logging into the correct region's Lost Ark site since regions are separate from one another. ### Step 3: Watch Participating Streamers Find streamers who are playing Lost Ark and have enabled Twitch Drops for their viewers. You can check if a streamer is participating by looking for a notification banner on their channel page or by asking them directly via chat. Keep in mind that not all streamers choose to participate in the Twitch Drop program. ### Step 4: Connect Your Steam Account (Optional) If you plan on playing Lost Ark via Steam, connect your Steam account to both your Lost Ark and Twitch accounts. This extra step guarantees seamless integration of your Twitch Drops into the game once it becomes available on Steam. To do so, find the respective "Link Steam" buttons within your Lost Ark and Twitch account settings. ### Step 5: Claim Your Drops When you become eligible for a drop during a qualifying streaming session, a pop-up message should appear on your screen prompting you to claim it. Be aware that some drops require multiple viewing sessions before they become claimable. If you miss claiming a drop while watching live, fear not! Unclaimed drops remain accessible for up to 24 hours after they first became available. Navigate to your Twitch inventory to view and claim uncollected items. ### Step 6: Check Your Lost Ark Inventory After successfully connecting your accounts and collecting twitch drops, launch Lost Ark and check your in-game mailbox or inventory for the new items. Note that certain drops may only be usable upon reaching specific levels or progression points within the game. #### Key Tips & Guidelines * Always ensure your linked accounts are set to the same region. Mixing regions might cause issues when trying to collect your Twitch Drops. * Remember that dropping rates vary among streamers; thus, switching channels could potentially increase your chances of receiving a drop. * Stay vigilant during events as exclusive event-related drops often carry higher value than regular ones. * Support content creators by chatting, following, and cheering for them - fostering positive relationships within the gaming community never hurts! By diligently following these steps and keeping our key tips in mind, you're now well-equipped to embark on your journey towards acquiring those coveted Lost Ark Twitch Drops! [as MEC helps MOT finish sticking shape on page] Title: Find Your Signature Look ============================= A signature look is a personal style that represents who you are and makes you feel confident. It's an intentional combination of clothing, accessories, makeup, and hairstyles that reflects your personality and suits your lifestyle. Finding your signature look can be a fun and rewarding journey! This detailed guide will help you discover and develop your unique style through introspection, research, experimentation, and refinement. ### Step 1: Define Your Style Personality The first step in finding your signature look is understanding your style personality-the adjectives that best describe your aesthetic preferences. To define your style personality, consider the following questions: * What words would you use to describe your dream closet? (e.g., chic, minimalist, bohemian, preppy) * Which fashion designers or brands do you admire most? Why? * Who are some celebrities or influencers whose styles inspire you? Analyze their looks to identify common themes. * When shopping, which sections do you gravitate towards first? Are they casual, formal, colorful, neutral, etc.? * Think about occasions where you felt most comfortable and stylish. Describe the outfits you wore during those times. By answering these questions, you should start recognizing patterns and recurring themes that represent your style personality. You may find yourself identifying with several categories; remember, there are no strict rules here. The goal is to create a cohesive mix of elements that feels authentic to you. ### Step 2: Evaluate Your Lifestyle Some places, such as churches have pictures on windows made out of colored pieces of glass. How to draw cartoon frogs Drawing cartoon frogs: some fun facts before starting sketching! - Some frogs don't have a tongue at all. - Usually, a frog will live around 6 to 12 years. - Frogs don't drink. They rather absorb water through their skin. How about drawing some frogs! This amphibian is well-known for his big eyeballs (1) and his strong back legs (3). Frogs are very often represent with a green skin (2) but they can be brown, blue or even red! Also notice the digits (4) of this animal. They are usually very thin and long with an oval shape at the end. Let's jump to the next step! Notice how this animal is well designed! The head is made of an oval shape. The snout is narrower while the part that connects with the body is wider. The body is made of a big circle. The leg on the back is strong and folded on itself. The forelimb is more delicate and more complex to draw. The digits always seems to be separated one from another. Let's try to draw a simple frog. First draw a circle for the body and an oval for the head. Next, draw the leg on the back and add the digits. Continue your drawing by adding the two little arms on the front. Draw a circle on the abdomen and add details like the mouth and the eyes. If you wish, you can add colors to create a more professional drawing. It's now time to experiment and try to draw different frogs. This one is rather easy to draw (2) and the result is really interesting. If you feel more comfortable, you can try to draw a more complex frog from a side view (1). Enjoy! Title: How to Get Your Parents to Do Something with You Introduction: Involving your parents in activities can be a rewarding experience that strengthens family bonds and creates shared memories. However, getting them to participate might sometimes feel challenging. This guide will provide you with practical steps and strategies to encourage your parents to engage in an activity with you. Remember, patience and understanding are essential as everyone has different interests and schedules. Step 1: Identify the Activity Choose an activity that suits both your interests and your parents' preferences. Consider their hobbies, physical abilities, and available time. By selecting an appropriate activity, you increase the likelihood of their participation. Key tip: Opt for something neither overly strenuous nor overwhelming, but engaging and enjoyable for all parties involved. Step 2: Plan Ahead Plan the activity well in advance. Give your parents ample notice so they can adjust their schedule accordingly. Provide them with necessary details such as date, time, location, duration, and any required materials or attire. Being organized demonstrates respect for their time and commitment. Guideline: Ideally, discuss the plan at least one week beforehand. Step 3: Communicate Effectively Express your desires clearly and respectfully when approaching your parents about the proposed activity. Use "I" statements instead of "you" statements to avoid sounding accusatory or demanding. For example, say, "I would really enjoy going for a bike ride together this weekend," rather than, "You never spend time with me." Key tip: Be open-minded and willing to negotiate alternative dates or activities if needed. Step 4: Highlight Benefits Emphasize the positive aspects of participating in the chosen activity together. Explain how it could benefit them physically, mentally, emotionally, or socially. Additionally, share how meaningful it would be for you to have this shared experience. Guideline: Avoid making exaggerated claims; instead focus on genuine benefits relevant to your parents' lifestyle and values. Step 5: Address Concerns Anticipate potential objections and address them proactively. If finances, transportation, or childcare are concerns, propose solutions or compromises. Showing initiative and problem-solving skills may alleviate doubts and apprehensions. Key tip: Display empathy and consider their perspective during discussions. Step 6: Follow Up After initially discussing the activity, periodically remind your parents without being pushy. Gently bring up the subject in conversation, expressing enthusiasm and anticipation. As the event approaches, confirm plans and clarify any remaining details. Guideline: Maintaining regular communication prevents forgetfulness and shows dedication. Step 7: Express Gratitude Once your parents agree to join you, thank them sincerely for their support and involvement. After completing the activity, reiterate your appreciation and share your favorite moments. Discuss what you enjoyed most and any lessons learned. Key tip: Make sure to acknowledge their effort and time investment graciously. Step 8: Encourage Future Participation To foster continued engagement, suggest future activities based on mutual interests. Offer options with varying levels of commitment, from weekly outings to annual trips. Demonstrating flexibility increases the chances of ongoing collaboration. Guideline: Gradually build upon initial successes by proposing new experiences tailored to individual preferences. Conclusion: By following these eight steps, you enhance the probability of involving your parents in shared activities. Patience, clear communication, and consideration remain crucial throughout the process. Keep in mind that building strong relationships takes time and consistent effort. Ultimately, investing energy into fostering connections results in deeper bonds and cherished memories. One morning he ate through one apple, but he was still hungry. Okay let's see what else Row has. Not until I talk to Carol Bro. Do you know what that's called? Well you sure do love your TV. A saxophone is a family of musical instruments that are made of brass. These are often just called a "sax". It has seven main keys, including the octave key. Because it is made from brass it is often included in the brass section alongside true brass instruments like the trumpet or trombone. However, it is not a true brass instrument but a member of the woodwind family of instruments because it has a reed. It was developed from the clarinet and shares many similarities to the clarinet. The player blows into a reed fitted into the mouthpiece of the instrument. Adolf Sax creates the instrument, but at first, it was heavily disliked because he boasted about it. Conductors would not add it to their orchestras because of the horrible reputations it had because of Adolf Sax. It started to gain popularity and lose that bad review, in the early 20th century. Key Fact 3: There are a number of basic food skills which enable us to cook a variety of dishes. a) To understand that there is a range of basic cooking skills. Explain to the children that we need certain skills to be able to make snacks and meals. Ask them to recall some of the skills they used last session when making their playtime snacks. - Cutting (with scissors). You could make a list of these or display the appropriate Actions Cards 205. Ask the children to think of other food skills which might be needed for cooking. What have they seen others do when cooking? Show some or all of the following videos: - Cous Cous Salad; - Fruit Salad; As you show each video, ask the children to identify 2-3 key skills needed for each recipe and keep a list, which can be seen by the whole class, of these skills under the heading of each recipe. You could use the Skills SMARTBoard activity 201 to help record this. The children should decide on 2 of the recipe videos they have seen and, using the, Cooking skills Worksheet 206, illustrate 2-3 key skills needed in those recipes. b) To be able to use basic cooking skills to make a dish. In advance of the lesson, decide on 1 or 2 non heat cooking activities that will require children to use a knife, e.g. sandwiches, fruit salad, dip. Organise the classroom, equipment and ingredients to allow a cooking session to take place. Plan how you will work with the children, e.g. half a class at a time. See Cooking Guide 205. Explain to the children what they will be making. Talk through the hygiene rules for cooking. Use the Hygiene and safety checklist Guide 204 for reference and display the Let's get ready to cook Poster 201 for the children to see. Talk through the recipe and demonstrate what the children will have to do. Be clear and precise when demonstrating how to use the knife in a safe and appropriate way. See Skills Guide 203. Demonstrate: - Bridge Hold; - Claw Grip; - Fork Secure. Use the Bridge Hold and Claw Grip Videos to reinforce good practice. Ensure children understand that when they finish cutting they must put the knife down. They should not hold it while they do other things or move around the room. This rule also applies to other sharp or pointed equipment. Allow the children to carry out their cooking activity. Gather the children to look at what they have made. Question them: - Did you enjoy making this? - Would you make it again? - Would you do anything differently? - What equipment did you use? - How did you use it safely? - What can you do now that you had not done before? Allow the children to taste what they have made. Remember to instruct the children on how to do this hygienically. See Tasting checklist Guide 200. Ask different children to come to the front of the class and mime a food skill, e.g. grating cheese, washing grapes. See if other children can guess what they are doing. For each skill guessed, ask if anyone can name another food this skill could be used with. Get the children to talk about the rules for tasting, being hygienic while cooking and using a knife safely. Use other non heat recipes which allow children to explore different food skills. To help create time for more food work, link it into other curriculum subjects. Common examples of elements that make these changes we call radioactivity are radium and uranium. An atom of uranium can lose an "alpha particle" (the atomic nucleus of helium) and become a new element with a lighter nucleus. Then that atom will emit two electrons, but it will not be stable yet. It will emit a series of alpha particles and electrons until it finally becomes the element Pb or what we call lead. By throwing out all these particles that have mass it has made its own mass smaller. It has also produced energy. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/18/5694MC.cha = = = Hey No are you ready to look at something different or are you still playing with those toys?
