Title: How to Contact Amazon Prime Customer Service Amazon Prime is a popular subscription service that offers its members benefits such as free two-day shipping, access to streaming video and music, and more. If you're an Amazon Prime member and need assistance with your account or have questions about the service, this tutorial will guide you through contacting Amazon Prime customer service. We'll cover various methods of getting help, from self-service options to speaking directly with a representative. ### Method 1: Utilizing Amazon's Help Center Before reaching out to a live agent, consider using Amazon's extensive online Help Center. It contains numerous articles, FAQs, and troubleshooting guides addressing common issues related to Amazon Prime. Here are some steps to navigate the Help Center effectively: **Step 1:** Visit Amazon's official website ([www.amazon.com](http://www.amazon.com)) and hover over the "Help" tab at the top right corner of the page. A dropdown menu should appear. **Key Tip**: Make sure you've signed into your Amazon account before proceeding so that the system can provide tailored support based on your account information and order history. **Why This Helps**: Accessing the Help Center first allows you to find quick solutions without waiting for an available representative. The Help Center also provides valuable resources like manuals, user guides, and community forum discussions. --- ### Method 2: Chat Support If you cannot resolve your issue via the Help Center, try chatting with a support agent. Live chat enables real-time communication while still providing written documentation of your conversation. **Step 1:** Navigate back to the "Help" tab as described earlier. From the dropdown menu, select "Need More Help?". You'll be redirected to a new page. **Step 2:** On the next page, scroll down until you see the question "Tell us more about your issue." Provide relevant details regarding your concern. Then click the orange button labeled "Start Chatting Now". ![Chat Button Example Image](https://storage.googleapis.com/wikihow-static-46f3d5989c6cc.appspot.com/fs/740e0_7b/en-us/ [BLA continues to scribble on the paper while watching MOT] Does he uh play with toys that make a lot of noise at home? Gandhi was born on October 2, 1869 in Porbandar, Gujarat, India. Several members of his family worked for the government of the state. When Gandhi was 18 years old, he went to study law in England. After he became a lawyer, he went to the British colony of South Africa where he experienced laws that said people with dark skin had fewer rights than people with light skin. In 1897, Gandhi was attacked by a group of people in Durban Harbor, South Africa when he was going to work. He went to South Africa because he could not find work in India. When traveling through South Africa, Gandhi was also kicked out of a first class train because of his skin color. Then Gandhi started protesting against segregation. He decided then to become a political activist, so he could help change these unfair laws. He created a powerful, non-violent movement. During Gandhi's life, India was a colony of the British Empire, but wanted independence. He was a huge leader during that era and his thoughts helped catalyze the Indian independence movement. Let's Read Fast!!! Independence and Fluency Fluent reading is reading where words are recognized easily and automatically. Readers must develop fluent reading in order to become faster, smoother, and more expressive readers. After a child is able to read fluently they can begin to develop their silent reading skills. Silent reading skills can only develop after the child has developed fluency in their reading. Fluent reading is extremely important in a child's overall reading development and success because it leads to better comprehension, which is the ultimate goal of reading. This lesson is designed to give students repeated readings of texts in order to become more fluent readers. In addition, it will help the students to understand the importance of automatic, fluent reading. -speed reading record for each student -Partner check sheet for each student -stop-watches for each pair of students -pencils for students -coverup buddies for each student (a popcicle stick with googley eyes glued to one end) - Copies of the book for each student or pair of students: Kite Day at Pine Lake by Sheila Cushman & Rona Kornblum. c1990. Speed Reading Record: - After 1st read _______ - After 2nd read _______ - After 3rd read _______ Partner Check Sheet for students to assess their partner's fluency: As I listened to my partner read, he/she: After 2nd After 3rd 1. Remembered more words _______ _______ 2. Read faster _______ _______ 3. Read smoother _______ _______ 4. Read with expression _______ _______ 1. Begin lesson by explaining to children that in order to become fluent readers they must learn to read fast and accuratly. In addition, they must remember what they have read in order to understanding the meaning of the text. Boys and girls, today we are going to practice reading with speed and accuracy. By learning to read with speed and accuracy we are going to learn to become fluent readers. It is important that we learn to read fluently so that we can read things quickly and easily without much effort. When we don't have to use a bunch of effort figuring out all the words, we can focus on the meaning of the words we are reading. Reading a story many times helps us to become fluent readers. Today we are going to read a story many times so that we can become more fluent readers. Each time you read, I want you to try to read faster. 2. Explain to the students the coverup strategy that they can use while reading. While reading, you may come across some troublesome words. A way to help you figure out the word, you can use your coverup buddy. Write the word thump on the board. Using my coverup buddy I am going to model how to decode a word. When you come across a tough word use the coverup buddy to cover up parts of the word so you can sound it out. Cover up all the letters except the u and sound out the sound of the short u=/u/. Then I am going to look at the letters leading up to the vowel, th=/th/. Finally I will focus on the m=/m/ and the p=/p/. So I am going to look at just the vowel, and I know that the short u makes and /u/ sound, remember like the tugboat. Next, I am going to look at the letters leading up to the vowel, so th=/th/ so I have /th//u/ and finally I look at the end of the word, so /m/ and /p/, so /th//u//m//p/. So now when you come across a tough word, remember your cover up buddy. 3. Now I am going to model fluent reading. I am going to write the following sentence on the board: The children love flying kites. Now I want each of you to listen as I read this sentence. The first time I am going to read it slowly without fluency. The---children---love---flying---kites. Now I am going to read it fluently and faster: The children love flying kites. Which one sounds better? Which one is easier to understand? It is much easier to listen to reading that is fluent and fast than reading that is slow and is not fluent. That is why we need to practice reading fluently and quickly so that when we read to each other it is easy to listen to and we can focus on the meaning of the words instead. 4. Now I am going to give each student a copy of the book Kite Day at Pine Lake. They will each get to read it by themselves before coming together with a partner for timed readings. I will give the following book talk to get the students interested in the story: This story is about a group of children who love to fly their kites at the lake. A young boy named Bob does not have his own kite and he is sad because he cannot fly kites with the other kids at the lake. All of their kites are wonderful shapes, colors, and sizes. Bob just wants a kite too. Will Bob end up getting a kite? Well, read to find out. After the students finish reading we will discuss the events of the story. 5. Next, the students will break up into partners and I will give each group a stopwatch and each child a Partner Check Sheet and Speed Reading Record. Each child will read the book three times. The listener will time each reading and give a report after the second and third readings. They will record the times of each reading on the Speed Reading Record. The reports are always complementary and do not put down a child. No criticism or advice is allowed. The child simply marks on the evaluation sheet. With your partner you will read the book three times. Time each reading and record the time on your Speed Reading Record. After the second and third readings, you will mark the evaluation sheet. You may look at the times to determine if your partner is reading faster each time. No put downs or criticisms about your partners reading is allowed. Only nice or encouraging words are allowed. This is a learning time for everyone, some people take a little longer than others and we don't want to hurt anyone's feelings. Assessment: The students will each bring me their Speed Reading Record and partner checklist. I will perform one minute reads with each child to check for fluency and accuracy. I will also evaluate their reading record and partner checklist by looking for improvement in reading time and improvement on the partner checklist. Murray, Bruce. Title: How to Care for a Pregnant Guinea Pig ========================================== A guinea pig pregnancy typically lasts between 59-72 days. During this time, she will require special care to ensure both her health and the health of her babies (known as pups). This guide will help you provide the best possible care for your pregnant guinea pig. ### Step 1: Confirm the Pregnancy Before taking any steps towards caring for a pregnant guinea pig, confirm that she is indeed expecting. Signs include weight gain, increased appetite, and a noticeable bulge in the abdomen after day 30. If unsure, consult with a vet who specializes in small animals. **Key Tip:** Regularly check your guinea pig's weight using a kitchen scale; an increase of approximately 80 grams per week could indicate pregnancy. The rule which has the letter O with a slash and the zero without was used at IBM and a few other early mainframe makers; this is even more of a problem for Scandinavians, because it looks like two of their letters at the same time. Some Burroughs/Unisys computers display a zero with a backwards slash. And yet another convention common on early line printers left zero without any extra dots or slashes but added a tail or hook to the letter O so that it resembled an inverted Q or cursive capital letter O. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/07/6510LC.cha = = = Assamese alphabet: , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , , We had peas last week, didn't we? Title: How to Access the Podcasts App and Its Widget on an iPhone The Podcasts app is a great way to stay up-to-date with your favorite shows and discover new ones. With the addition of the Today View widget, you can now access your podcast subscriptions and playlists even faster. This tutorial will guide you through the process of finding and using the Podcasts app and its associated widget on your iPhone. **Step 1: Locate the Podcasts app** Your first task is to find the native Podcasts application installed on every iOS device since version 8.4. It's likely preinstalled on your iPhone, but if you cannot locate it, search for "Podcasts" in Spotlight Search (accessible by swiping down from any home screen). If you don't have the app yet, download it from the Apple App Store. *Key tip:* If you still can't find the Podcasts app after searching, ensure that it hasn't been accidentally deleted or hidden inside a folder. **Step 2: Open the Podcasts app** Tap on the Podcasts icon to launch the application. The icon has a multicolored background and features a microphone image. Upon opening, you are greeted with several options such as Listen Now, Library, Browse, and Search at the bottom of the interface. These tabs allow easy navigation between various sections within the app. *Guideline*: Familiarize yourself with these main tabs to make better use of the Podcasts app. **Step 3: Navigate to Settings** To manage settings related to the Podcasts app, tap on 'Settings' found on your iPhone's home screen or via the Settings app. Scroll down until you see 'Podcasts', then select this option. Here, you may customize some aspects like episode download settings, notification preferences, and more. *Tip*: Adjusting these settings appropriately could improve user experience and save storage space. **Step 4: Add the Podcasts widget to Today View** Swipe right from the lock screen or the home screen to open Today View. At the bottom of today view, click on 'Edit'. A list of available apps with their respective widgets appears; scroll down and look for 'Podcasts.' Tap the green "+Add Widget" button next to it. Afterward, press 'Done' located in the upper right corner. You should now see the Podcasts widget added above other widgets in Today View. *Note*: Depending upon your iOS version, the location and appearance of adding widgets might slightly vary. **Step 5: Interact with the Podcasts widget** In the Today View, interact with the newly placed Podcasts widget directly without having to enter the full app. You can quickly resume playing episodes, browse top charts, or explore curated collections based on genres or topics. Moreover, clicking 'Manage Shows' lets you choose which shows appear in the compact format of the widget. *Guideline*: Customizing what displays on the widget saves time when deciding which podcast to listen to next. By following these steps, you have successfully accessed both the Podcasts app and its corresponding widget on your iPhone. Enjoy quicker access to all things podcast-related while taking advantage of additional functionality provided by the handy Today View feature! When the small band of freedom fighters. Title: Adding More Effects, Overlays, and Borders in Pixlr-o-matic on Android 1. **Open the Pixlr-o-matic app:** To begin adding more effects, overlays, and borders to your photos, first open the Pixlr-o-matic app on your Android device. Familiarize yourself with the layout; you will see options for selecting an image, applying effects, adjusting intensity levels, and saving or sharing your creation. 2. **Select an Image:** Tap on the '+' icon at the bottom center of the screen. This will take you to your photo gallery where you can choose an existing picture or capture a new one using your camera. Once selected, the image will load within the Pixlr-o-matic interface. 3. **Understanding Layers:** Before we dive into adding effects, overlays, and borders, let's understand layers. There are three types of layers available - effect, overlay, and border. Each layer type has its unique set of filters and styles that can be applied individually or combined for various outcomes. You can rearrange these layers as needed by dragging them up or down. 4. **Applying Effects:** Tap on the 'effect' tab (it looks like a film strip). Scroll through numerous categories such as color, vintage, retro, etc., then select any category to view individual filters under it. Choose a filter by tapping on it once. Notice how this changes the overall mood and tone of your image. Key tip here is experimentation - try out different combinations until you find something satisfying. Remember, you can always reset back to original if required. 5. **Adjusting Intensity Levels:** After choosing an effect, notice the slider bar below the effect thumbnail. Drag it left or right to increase or decrease the intensity level respectively. Adjusting intensity allows fine tuning of the chosen effect so that it blends well with the rest of the image. 6. **Adding Overlays:** Move onto the 'overlay' tab now (represented by a brush symbol). Similar to effects, there are several categories including grunge, light leak, texture, etc. Selecting an overlay adds another dimension to your image creating depth and interest. For instance, picking a light leak overlay can simulate sunlight entering from an edge giving a warm feel. Again, play around with intensities for best results. 7. **Applying Borders:** Lastly, head over to the 'border' tab denoted by a frame symbol. Here, you'll discover diverse frames ranging from classic Polaroid shapes to stylish modern designs. Applying a border finalizes your edit, providing a neat finish. Just like before, tweak the width setting via the slider according to preference. 8. **Reordering Layers:** At any point during editing, should you wish to modify the order of application for layers, simply press and hold the specific layer icon, then move it accordingly above or below others. Reorganizing layers affects the end result significantly, making this feature crucial when perfecting edits. 9. **Saving & Sharing Your Creation:** Upon being content with your edited masterpiece, save it either locally on your device or share directly via social media platforms supported within the app. Hit the 'Save' button located at top right corner, pick between Save Photo or Share, and proceed. By following these steps meticulously, you've successfully added multiple effects, overlays, and borders to enhance your photograph using Pixlr-o-matic on your Android device! Until the mid 20th century, beef cattle were often sent to market "on the hoof". Cowboys or drovers would herd the cattle along the roads or on trails to the cattle markets in big towns or cities, or to railway stations where they would be loaded and shipped to these towns or cities. In Australia, sometimes the cattle would travel for hundreds of miles along roads known as 'Traveling Stock Routes'. Big herds would have thousands of 'heads of cattle'. (Cattle are counted by the "head".) Nowadays cattle are usually sent to the market in huge lorries known as road-trains. In North America, cattle are sent to auction marts, slaughter plants or other farms or ranches by large semi-trucks called cattle liners. Um do you play the game in the Karen Katz book? The objects pictured above are interesting structures - they are derived from the prime factorization of a given number n. They can be described in a number of ways - for example, as directed graphs. Because they are nicely structured, they actually form something more special - a lattice. Accordingly, these structures are called factor lattices. It's easy to start drawing these by hand following the instructions below. 1. The first node is 1 2. Draw arrows out of this node for each of the prime factors of n. 3. The arrows that you just drew should connect to nodes labled with the prime factors of n. Now, for each of the new nodes that you drew do the following: 4. Start from a node x that is not equal to n. 5. Draw arrows out of this node for each of the prime factors of n/x. 6. The arrows that you just drew (one for each p = n/x) should connect to nodes labled with the numbers p*x. 7. Now repeat 4,5, and 6 for each new node that you have drawn that is not equal to n. This process is recursive, and ends when you have the complete lattice. The process is well suited for implementation as a computer program - the images above were created using SAGE using output from a Java program based on the algorithm above. Manually trying out the steps out for a number like n = 24 goes something like this: First write out the prime factorization of 24, 24=(2*2*2)*3 = (2^3)*3. Starting with 1, draw arrows out to 2 and 3. Now looking at each node and following the algorithm, from the 2 you will get arrows out to 4 and 6. From the 3 you will get an arrow out to 6 as well. From 4 you will get arrows out to 8 or 12. From 6 you will get an arrow out to 12 as well. From 8 and from 12 you get arrows out to 24, and you are done. In general, the algorithm produces a lattice that can be described as follows. Each node is a factor of the given number n. Two nodes are connected by an edge if their prime factorization differs by a single prime number. In other words, if a and b are nodes, and p = b/a, then there is an arrow p:a-->b. It's a good exercise to make the connections between the lattice structure and the prime factorization of a number n. 1. What does the factor lattice of a prime number look like? 2. If a number is just a power of a prime, what does its lattice look like? 3. If you know the factorization, can you find the number of nodes without drawing the lattice. The answer to the last question (3) can be expressed as: For example, if n = 24= 2^3*3, then the number of nodes will be (3+1)(1+1) = 8 That these structures can be thought of as "lattices"comes from the fact that you can think of the arrows as an ordering of the nodes, ab. The number 1 is always the least node in the factor lattice for n, while n itself is the greatest node. The property that actually makes these structures a "lattice" is that for any two nodes there is always a lower-bound for any pair of nodes in the lattice, and always an upper-bound for the pair (these are often referred to as meets and joins). The Wolfram Demonstrations Project has a nice factor lattice demo that will draw factor lattices for a large number of integers for you. There is also a good Wikipedia entry for lattices in general. [blows her nose then puts the used tissue in her mouth] Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emily. She was three years old, and she liked finding new things. One day, Emily went on a walk with her mom. They were in the garden, and Mom said, "Look, Emily! What do you see?" "I see a zipper!" said Emily excitedly. Which animal do you think you want to ride on? John thought hey this is a bad idea. Food Safety at Home 4 Basic Steps to Food Safety at Home Always wash your food, hands, counters and cooking tools. Wash hands in warm soapy water for at least 20 seconds. Do this before and after touching food. Wash your cutting boards, dishes, forks, spoons, knives and counter tops with hot soapy water. Do this after working with each food item. Rinse fruits and veggies. Clean the lids on canned goods before opening. 2. Separate (Keep Apart) Keep raw foods to themselves. Germs can spread from one food to another. Keep raw meat, poultry, seafood, and eggs away from other foods. Do this in your shopping cart, bags, and fridge. Do not reuse marinades used on raw foods unless you bring them to a boil first. Use a special cutting board or plate for raw foods only. Foods need to get hot and stay hot. Heat kills germs. Cook to safe temperatures: - Beef, Pork, Lamb 145 F - Fish 145 F - Ground Beef, Pork, Lamb 160 F - Turkey, Chicken, Duck 165 F Use a food thermometer to make sure that food is done. You can't always tell by looking. Put food in the fridge right away. 2-Hour Rule: Put foods in the fridge or freezer within 2 hours after cooking or buying from the store. Do this within 1 hour if it is 90 degrees or hotter outside. Never thaw food by simply taking it out of the fridge. - In the fridge - Under cold water - In the microwave Marinate foods in the fridge. Food Safety at Home Why should you care about food safety? Each year millions of people get sick from food illnesses. Food illness can cause you to feel like you have the flu. Food illness can also cause serious health problems, even death. Think you have a food illness? Call your doctor and get medical care right away. Save the food package, can or carton. Call USDA at 1-888-674-6854 for meat, poultry or eggs. Call FDA at 1-866-300-4374 for all other foods. Call your local health department if you think you got sick from food you ate in a restaurant or other food seller. Who is at risk? Anyone can get sick from eating spoiled food. Some people are more likely to get sick from food illnesses. -People with certain health conditions like cancer, HIV/AIDS, diabetes and kidney disease Some foods are more risky for these people. Talk to your doctor or other health provider about which foods are safe for you to eat. The so-called "1964 1/2" Mustang was designed by Joe Oros and Dave Ash of Ford's Mercury division, was based on another Ford car, the Falcon and was proposed by Ford's president, Lee Iacocca. The Falcon was not expensive to build and that helped Iacocca get the Mustang the approval it needed to be built. The people who ran the Ford company did not want to take a chance on a car like that because they lost a lot of money on another car, the Edsel, just a few years back. It set sales records and became one of the best selling cars of all time. It would remain mostly the same until 1966. So popular was the Mustang that three Ford assembly plants worked to build it. Title: How to Be a Warrior Cat in Animal Jam Introduction: Animal Jam is an online playground where players can adopt and personalize their own animal characters, create dens, chat with friends, explore the world, participate in games and activities, and learn about wildlife and environmental conservation. This tutorial will focus on creating a warrior cat persona within the game, based on the popular book series by Erin Hunter. By following these steps, you'll be able to role-play as a member of a wild cat clan, complete with accurate clothing, poses, names, and behaviors. Step 1: Choose Your Animal In Animal Jam, there are various animals available that could potentially serve as your warrior cat character. However, only certain species are typically associated with warrior cats - such as lions, tigers, leopards, cheetahs, cougars, snow leopards, servals, lynxes, and bobcats. Select one of these animals to begin building your warrior cat identity. The choice of animal may reflect which clan or group your character belongs to (e.g., choosing a lion for the LionClan). Key Tip: Consider selecting an animal whose appearance closely resembles the description of a Clan cat from the books. For example, ThunderClan members have darker fur colors like gray, brown, black, and white, while RiverClan cats tend to have lighter, sleeker coats like blue, silver, and cream. Step 2: Customize Your Appearance Once you've chosen your base animal, head over to the customization menu to tailor its appearance to fit your ideal warrior cat look. Focus on details like scars, ear tufts, color patterns, and eye shapes. Try to mimic wounds and markings commonly found among warrior cats in the books. If possible, avoid using bright colors or flashy accessories uncharacteristic of wild cats. Keeping your design simple yet authentic adds credibility to your character. Key Guideline: To maintain realism, stick to natural-looking patterns and muted colors. Avoid neon hues and excessive embellishments. Also, consider adding battle scars to give your character a more rugged, experienced appearance. Step 3: Name Your Character Warrior cat names generally consist of two parts: a prefix representing the character's gender and rank, followed by a suffix denoting their individual personality trait or birth order. Here's a list of common prefixes and suffixes taken directly from the books: Prefixes: * Male: Fire, Cloud, Storm, Black, Gray, Snow, Jag, Swoop, Mud, etc. * Female: Bright, Dust, Frost, Fern, Blossom, Misty, Holly, Mouse, Spottedleaf, etc. Suffixes: Once upon a time there lived an ancient rat. The rat wanted to study so he went looking for a teacher. He met a friendly cat and asked "Will you teach me?" The cat smiled and said "Yes, I'd be happy to teach you." The rat was so excited. He said "Thank you! Will you teach me lots?" The cat answered "Yes, I'll teach you lots." Together the cat and rat studied every day. They read books and played games. The rat learned so much and was so happy. One day the cat said to the rat "You have learned so much. You are now ready to go and share all that you have learned with the world." The rat smiled and said "Thank you for everything! I will always remember my teacher." The rat waved goodbye with a smile and left to share his knowledge with the world. Title: How to ConfigureRIP (Routing Information Protocol) Introduction: Routing Information Protocol (RIP) is a distance-vector routing protocol used to automatically share route information between routers within an autonomous system (AS). It uses hop count as its metric to determine the best path to a destination network. This tutorial will guide you through configuring RIP on Cisco IOS devices using the command line interface (CLI). By following these steps, you'll be able to successfully set up RIP to exchange routes with neighboring routers and establish efficient communication paths across your network. Step 1: Access Device Console or SSH ------------------------------------- First, connect to your device via console cable or secure shell (SSH). Once connected, access privileged mode by entering the enable command followed by the appropriate password. Why this matters: To configure any settings on a router, you must first gain access to privileged mode, which allows full configuration capabilities. Key tip: Always ensure that your connection is secured when connecting remotely to avoid unauthorized access. Use strong passwords and consider implementing encryption methods such as SSH or Virtual Private Networks (VPNs). Step 2: Enter Global Configuration Mode -------------------------------------- To begin making changes to the overall device configuration, enter global configuration mode using the 'configure terminal' command. You should see the prompt change from `Router>` to `Router(config)#`. Why this matters: Making changes directly in privileged mode affects all interfaces without specifying them individually; however, most configurations need to be applied per interface. Therefore, we require global configuration mode to define parameters affecting the entire device before moving onto individual interfaces. Step 3: Enable RIP Version 2 ---------------------------- By default, RIP operates in version 1 mode. However, it is recommended to use RIPv2 due to its support for VLSMs (Variable Length Subnet Masks), authentication, and other enhancements over RIPv1. Issue the command `version 2` under global configuration mode. Why this matters: Enabling RIPv2 ensures compatibility with modern networks while providing additional features compared to RIPv1. Step 4: Define Networks to Advertise ----------------------------------- Next, specify the networks you want to advertise using RIP by issuing the `router rip` command, followed by the `network` keyword along with the IP addresses of the interfaces participating in RIP. For example, if there are two Ethernet interfaces configured with subnets 192.168.0.0/24 and 10.0.0.0/24 respectively, use the commands below: ```ruby router rip network 192.168.0.0 network 10.0.0.0 = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Soderstrom/The/000915c.cha = = = Was that the one [ball] that just rolled? Creating a Snuggly Hamster Bed Hamsters are cute, cuddly pets that require a cozy and safe environment to live in. One essential item your hamster needs is a comfortable bed where they can rest and sleep. This tutorial will guide you through creating a snuggly hamster bed using soft materials that are safe for your pet. Materials Needed: 1. Fabric scraps (preferably made from natural fibers like cotton or flannel) 2. Scissors 3. Thread and needle or sewing machine 4. Polyester stuffing or other hypoallergenic filling material 5. Measuring tape 6. Optional: Velcro or buttons for closure Step 1: Measure Your Hamster's House Before starting, measure the size of your hamster's house or cage to determine the appropriate size for the bed. The bed should be big enough for your hamster to curl up inside but small enough to fit comfortably in their living space. A good rule of thumb is to make the bed around 8-10 inches wide by 6-8 inches long. Key Tip: Make sure the bed is not too large as this could cause stress for your hamster. They may feel vulnerable if there is too much open space around them while sleeping. Step 2: Cut Out Two Identical Pieces of Fabric Using the measurements taken in Step 1, cut out two identical pieces of fabric. These will serve as the top and bottom layers of the bed. Be sure to choose fabrics free from chemicals or dyes that could harm your hamster. Natural fiber fabrics such as cotton or flannel are ideal because they are breathable and absorbent. Step 3: Sew the Edges Together With right sides facing each other, align the edges of both fabric pieces. Using either a sewing machine or hand stitching with a needle and thread, sew three sides together leaving about a half inch seam allowance. Leave one short side unsewn; this will serve as the opening for turning the fabric right side out and adding the filling. Step 4: Turn the Fabric Right Side Out Carefully turn the fabric inside out so that the seams are hidden within the bed. Use a blunt tool like a knitting needle or chopstick to poke out corners and ensure they are fully turned right side out. Step 5: Add Filling Material Fill the bed with polyester stuffing or another hypoallergenic filling material until it reaches the desired level of firmness. It should be plush but still supportive. Overfilling can lead to discomfort for your hamster. Step 6: Close the Opening Once filled, fold under the raw edges of the remaining unsewn side and hand-stitch it closed. Alternatively, you can attach Velcro or buttons here for easy removal and washing. Step 7: Place the Bed in Your Hamster's Cage Place the completed hamster bed into your hamster's cage. Ensure it fits securely without tipping over or moving around excessively. If necessary, add some non-toxic adhesive to keep it stable. Key Guideline: Always monitor your hamster during its first few days with the new bed to ensure they take well to it. Some hamsters might need time to get used to changes in their environment. By following these steps, you have now created a snuggly hamster bed perfect for providing your little friend with a cozy place to snooze! That have I donen't know a lazy eye or somethin'. The "number" zero (as in the "zero brothers" example above) is not the same as the "numeral" or "digit" zero, used in numeral systems using positional notation. Successive positions of digits have higher values, so the digit zero is used to skip a position and give appropriate value to the preceding and following digits. A zero digit is not always necessary in a different positional number system. Something called bijective numeration is a possible example of a system without zeroes. The very first wits and men of letters and statesmen and savans were enrolled in it, and each brought to the entertainment some choice morceau which he had laid aside from his own particular field to add to the feast. Look at a bird feather close up sometime. It is really an amazing part of a bird's anatomy, made to serve many functions. The image shown at right in Figure 1 is that of a contour feather, the largest of the feathers you'll find on a bird. It is labeled with its basic parts to help you understand how complex it really is. Commonly called the quill, it is the smooth, non-colored part of the feather shaft, which extends under the skin. The portion of the central shaft that rises above the skin. It is the base from which the barbs extend. The part of the feather that has barbs. Set of fibers, which come off the rachis at a 45-degree angle (see Figure 2). Very small fibers found on the barb. Barbules from one barb cross those of the next at a 90-degree angle (see Figure 2). Barbules have these, to hook the barbules together, like a zipper, forming a tight, smooth surface. Some feathers have a small growth at the base of the vane, which have barbs but no hooks. For tens of millions of years, during the Carboniferous and early Permian, amphibia were top predators on land, especially in the low-lying tropical river systems. In drier conditions, they were less effective, and the ancestors of mammals and reptiles (the Synapsids and Sauropsids) gradually took over the land. [as DUP places toys in the box] Auxins are plant growth hormones. They work by making cells grow longer, and divide more. A growth reaction to a stimulus is called a tropism, and different prefixes denote what type of stimulus is involved. Below is a diagram showing how a phototropism causes the plant to grow in the direction of the sun. The auxin collects on the dark side of the shoot; therefore, more growth will occur on this side of the plant. Therefore, the shoot will gradually lean towards the sun and it can now make best use of the environment. This very affect can be seen with house plants. If you have a plant in a room with only one window, it will often develop more on the side that faces this window. Plant hormones have many commercial uses, relating to agriculture and flower growing. Roots: A cutting from a plant can be taken, for example a branch. If a bit of auxin is applied to the base and this is put in soil, it will grow roots and a clone is made. Fruits. They can be used to slow down the process of ripening so they arrive fresh after transport. Weeds. As well as encouraging life, auxin can also be used to destroy it. Applying auxins can make weeds grow too fast and therefore die. Parents and teachers hear this often. What children don't always understand is that treating students fairly does not mean treating them all alike. Even adults sometimes say that fairness means everyone gets the same thing. Following that reasoning, if one person in the family gets a wheelchair, then everyone should get one. That, of course, is ridiculous! Only the one who needs a wheelchair gets one. If a child in my classroom cannot read a test for herself, then I provide a way for her to have the test read to her. If another cannot write the answers, then I provide a way to have the answers written for him. The child does the thinking for himself, but a skill deficit or physical problem should not keep him from succeeding in my class. This is treating students fairly-giving them what they need. On the other hand, if a child can read and write for herself, it would not be fair to read and write for her. She would not be getting to practice her reading and writing skills, nor would she continue to be independent. I always need to teach about fairness versus equal treatment when it relates to accommodations in the classroom. A blog post by Richard Curwin, "Fair Isn't Equal: Seven Classroom Tips," discusses the concept as it relates to behavior management. One thing Dr. Curwin says in that blog is that you should teach the concept of fairness before you implement it, which is an excellent idea. Next time you hear "That's not fair!" use the opening to have an important talk about fairness. Ask "What would be fair?" and go from there! More on this topic from Livia McCoy: Fairness Is Not Always What it Seems. A programmer usually tries to write instructions in small, related groups. That way, they can keep track of fewer changes in the program. But, that means the code may go on several pages. When a compiler sees that the target program uses several pages to explain the whole recipe, it may use a linker. The linker will put instructions that say where to find the code that's next. Finally, the compiler writes down the instructions in the target language. Do you see Dada's deer on here? And you know what the police did? Almost all species of bamboo have hollow stems divided into nodes or joints. The stem can be up to 30 cm (a foot) in diameter. Each of the node has one side bud. Not all of those buds develop into branches, but some do. This makes bamboo one of the few grasses that have a branch structure. Bamboo rarely flowers. Some species only flower once, and then die off. The distance of two joints in a bamboo is the basis of a traditional Japanese unit of measurement, "shaku". Annan became Secretary-General of the United Nations in January 1997, succeeding Boutros Boutros-Ghali of Egypt. Annan was a U.N. veteran who took his first job with the organization in 1962 and worked his way up through various posts including Deputy Director to the U.N. High Commissioner for Refugees (1980-83) and head of the U.N.'s peacekeeping efforts (1995-96). Annan was the first Secretary-General chosen from the ranks of the U.N.'s staff. He is also the first black man to hold the post and the second African (after Boutros-Ghali). His second term began in 2002 and ran through the end of 2006. Title: Eliminate Age Spots: A Comprehensive Guide Age spots, also known as liver spots or sunspots, are flat, brown or black patches that appear on areas of the skin frequently exposed to the sun. While age spots are generally harmless, they can make you look older than your actual age. Fortunately, there are several methods available to eliminate these unwanted blemishes without causing any significant damage to your health. Title: How to Dry Okra Seeds Introduction: Okra is a versatile vegetable that can be used in various dishes, from stews to pickles. Drying okra seeds is an excellent way to preserve them for future planting or cooking purposes. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drying okra seeds, providing detailed explanations and helpful tips along the way. Materials Needed: * Fresh okra pods * Colander or strainer * Paper towels * Airtight container or envelope * Marker (for labeling) Step 1: Selecting Suitable Pods Select fresh, mature okra pods with fully developed seeds inside. The ideal stage for harvesting okra for seed saving is when the pods are slightly overripe - they should feel firm but have started to turn pale green or yellowish. Avoid using soft, damaged, or diseased pods as this may lead to poor-quality seeds. Key Tip: Harvest your okra pods carefully by cutting them off at their base instead of pulling them away from the plant. Pulling can damage both the parent plant and its root system. Step 2: Cleaning the Pods Rinse the collected okra pods thoroughly under cool running water to remove any dirt or debris attached to the surface. Gently rub the pods together during rinsing to ensure proper cleaning. Afterward, place them in a colander or strainer and allow excess water to drain. Step 3: Drying the Pods Place the cleaned okra pods onto paper towels, ensuring there's enough space between each one so air can circulate freely around them. Allow them to dry naturally at room temperature for about two weeks, turning them occasionally to promote even drying. Once dried, the outer shells of the pods should appear shriveled and brittle. Guideline: If you live in a humid climate, consider speeding up the drying process by placing the okra pods near a fan or dehumidifier. However, avoid exposing them directly to sunlight as this might reduce their viability. Step 4: Extracting the Seeds Once the pods are completely dry, crack open their hard shells gently to extract the seeds. Discard any discolored, misshapen, or undersized seeds since these won't germinate well. Store only high-quality seeds in your chosen container. Key Tip: To make opening the pods easier, you can crush them lightly before attempting to split them apart. Remember to handle the seeds delicately throughout this process to prevent damaging them. Step 5: Storing the Seeds Transfer the extracted and sorted seeds into an airtight container such as a resealable plastic bag, glass jar, or envelope. Make sure all moisture has been removed from the storage container before sealing it tightly. Label the containers clearly with information like 'okra seeds,' year of collection, and any other relevant details. Guideline: For optimal longevity, store your dried okra seeds in a cool, dark, and dry location. Ideally, aim for temperatures below 70 F (21 C). Under suitable conditions, properly dried okra seeds can remain viable for up to four years! Conclusion: Drying okra seeds requires patience but offers significant rewards in terms of preservation and culinary uses. By following our comprehensive guide, we hope you now possess the knowledge required to confidently undertake this task yourself. Happy gardening and cooking! You should reach them you don't think? Title: How to Dress Like a Skater Girl Introduction: Skater girls have a unique and cool style that is inspired by skateboarding culture. This fashion trend has been popularized by many celebrities and influencers who embrace this laid-back yet edgy look. The following steps will guide you through dressing like a skater girl with confidence and authenticity. Remember, the most important aspect of any style is expressing yourself and feeling comfortable in your clothing choices. Step 1: Choose Casual Bottoms One of the defining features of skater girl style is casual bottoms such as jeans, shorts, or sweatpants. These pieces are often loose fitting, which allows for ease of movement while skating or simply lounging around. Opt for distressed denim, bold colors, or graphic prints to add some personality to your outfit. Key tip: Look for pants with multiple pockets for functionality and utility. Step 2: Select Comfortable Shoes Footwear plays an essential role in completing the skater girl aesthetic. Canvas sneakers, Vans slip-ons, Converse All Stars, or high-top shoes are all great options for achieving this look. Not only do these styles complement the relaxed vibe of the overall ensemble but they also provide adequate support and traction needed for skateboarding activities. Guideline: Prioritize comfort without compromising style when selecting footwear. Step 3: Pick Out Tops With Graphic Designs Graphic tees, tank tops, and hoodies featuring bold logos, band names, or quirky slogans are staples in a skater girl's wardrobe. These items can be layered over tighter shirts, bralettes, or sports bras depending on personal preference and weather conditions. Additionally, flannel button-downs tied at the waist offer both warmth and visual interest. Key tip: Consider shopping at thrift stores or online marketplaces specializing in vintage apparel for affordable and unique finds. Step 4: Accessorize Thoughtfully Accessories can elevate any outfit, including those adhering to the skater girl aesthetic. Beanies, snapbacks, or bucket hats serve dual purposes by protecting against sun exposure and adding street cred to your appearance. Thin gold chains, choker necklaces, or statement earrings introduce subtle femininity into the otherwise tomboyish attire. Backpacks or crossbody bags made from durable materials like canvas or leather ensure functionality without detracting from the overall design. Guideline: When choosing accessories, aim for pieces that reflect your individual taste while staying true to the skater girl ethos. Step 5: Embrace Individual Expression The beauty of skater girl style lies in its versatility and adaptability to various interpretations. Don't shy away from incorporating elements of other subcultures or trends into your outfits if they resonate with you personally. By blending influences and making thoughtful decisions about what feels right for you, you can create a genuinely unique and authentic representation of the skater girl aesthetic. Key tip: Confidence is the ultimate accessory - own your look and let it shine! Conclusion: In summary, mastering the art of dressing like a skater girl involves embracing casual bottoms, comfortable shoes, graphic tees, and functional accessories. However, remember that personal expression should always take precedence over blindly following trends. As you experiment with different combinations and develop your signature style, stay open to inspiration from diverse sources and never forget the importance of feeling confident and comfortable in your clothes. The Parliament serves as the legislative branch of government. Members of Parliament (MPs) are made up of elected, non-constituency and nominated members. Elected MPs are voted into parliament on a "first-past-the-post" (plurality) system and represent either single-member or group-representation constituencies. Elections are free, and the People's Action Party has won control of Parliament with large majorities in every election since self-governance in 1959. In the most recent parliamentary elections in 2020, the largest opposition, led by the Workers' Party, increased its representation in the House to 10 elected MPs out of 93. May I see the doggy that Maggie gave you? Hello young mathematicians! Today, we are going to learn about limits and how they relate to something called "definite integrals." Don't worry if those words sound complicated - by the end of this explanation, you will have a better understanding of them! Let's start with the concept of a limit. Imagine you are trying to get closer and closer to a wall. At first, you stand far away, but then you take one step forward. You are now closer than before, but still not very close. So, you take another step forward, getting even closer. This process continues until eventually, you are standing right up against the wall. The idea of taking smaller and smaller steps until you reach the wall is similar to finding a limit. In math, we often talk about finding the value of a function as we get infinitely close to a certain number or point. Now let's move on to definite integrals. Have you ever heard of adding lots of small numbers together to find a bigger one? That's essentially what integration does, but instead of just regular numbers, we work with functions. A definite integral has two specific values (called bounds) between which we want to integrate our function. It tells us exactly how much of the area under the curve of the function lies within these bounds. In the problem mentioned earlier, there is a limit expression involving sine and x, and it asks us to express the limit as a definite integral over the interval [0, 1]. What does this mean? Well, imagine graphing y = sin(x)/(1 + x). As we make n increasingly large, the points on the graph become more concentrated near x = 0 and x = 1. By converting this limit into a definite integral, we aim to calculate the total accumulation of all these tiny bits of areas underneath the curve, confined within the interval [0, 1]. While learning about calculus may seem challenging initially, remember that each new idea builds upon previous knowledge, making everything clearer along the way. Keep practicing, ask questions when needed, and enjoy your mathematical journey! These Asian elephants are being rounded up to be trained for work. This is the side for the door. Alright, think Thomas can wear shorts tomorrow? The unit of volume in the International System of Units is the cubic meter, which is represented by the symbol m. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy loved to play sport with his friends. They would run and jump and throw balls. One day, Timmy and his friends went to the park to play. Title: How to Clean a Cat's Litter Box Introduction: A clean litter box is essential for your cat's health and happiness. Cats are fastidious creatures and may avoid using a dirty litter box, which can lead to messy accidents around the house. Regular cleaning ensures that your feline friend remains healthy and your home stays fresh. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of cleaning a cat's litter box, providing detailed steps, useful tips, and best practices. What You'll Need: - Litter scoop or slotted spoon - Garbage bags - Paper towels or reusable cloths - Mild dish soap or unscented laundry detergent - Disinfectant (optional) - Gloves (recommended) - Optional: lint roller, vacuum cleaner, air freshener Step 1: Scoop Solid Waste Daily Desired Outcome: Removing solid waste daily reduces odor buildup and makes deep cleaning easier. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Put on gloves before starting this task to keep your hands clean. - Use a sturdy scoop or slotted spoon designed specifically for removing cat litter clumps. - Remove all visible feces and urine clumps from the litter box daily. - Dispose of the waste in a garbage bag and tie it securely before throwing it away. Step 2: Replace Litter Weekly Desired Outcome: Changing the litter weekly prevents bacterial growth and maintains a pleasant smell. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Empty the entire contents of the litter box into a garbage bag. - Add an appropriate amount of new litter according to the manufacturer's recommendations or your cat's preference. - Mix the new litter with any remaining old litter to help familiarize your cat with the new scent. Step 3: Deep Clean Monthly Desired Outcome: Thoroughly washing and disinfecting the litter box monthly eliminates bacteria, parasites, and unpleasant smells. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Take the litter box outside or to a well-ventilated area like a bathtub or shower. - Scrub the inside surfaces of the litter box thoroughly with warm water, mild dish soap, or unscented laundry detergent. Pay extra attention to corners and edges where residue might accumulate. - Rinelyse the litter box completely to remove soap suds and debris. - Optionally, spray or wipe down the litter box with a pet-safe disinfectant. Allow it to dry before refilling with litter. Additional Maintenance Tips: - Invest in multiple litter boxes if possible. Having at least one spare allows you to rotate them while cleaning, ensuring there is always a clean option available for your cat. - Trim your cat's nails regularly to minimize scratch marks on the sides of the litter box. - Consider using liners or baking soda under the litter to absorb moisture and reduce odors between cleanings. - Vacuum or sweep around the litter box frequently to control dust and scattered litter. - If necessary, use an air freshener or deodorizing spray designed for pets to neutralize lingering smells. - Monitor your cat's usage patterns and consult a veterinarian if they suddenly start avoiding their litter box as this could be a sign of illness. Title: How to Feed a Pregnant Dog Shortly Before Labor Introduction: Proper nutrition is crucial for pregnant dogs, especially during the final stages of pregnancy leading up to labor. A well-nourished mother canine will have the energy required for childbirth, nursing her puppies, and recovering from delivery. This article will guide you through the process of feeding your pregnant dog shortly before labor with clear steps, expert advice, and useful tips. Step 1: Determine the Stage of Pregnancy Consult your veterinarian to confirm your dog's pregnancy and estimate the due date. Generally, pregnancies last between 57 to 65 days. Adjustments to diet should be made progressively throughout this period; however, focus on high-quality food and increased portions during the final three weeks. Step 2: Choose High-Quality Food Select a high-quality dog food that meets AAFCO (Association of American Feed Control Officials) standards for gestating bitches. The food should contain at least 28% protein and 17% fat. Opt for wet or canned food if your dog has difficulty eating dry kibble due to dental issues or decreased appetite. Key tip: Avoid people food as they may lack essential nutrients for canines and could introduce harmful bacteria like Salmonella. Step 3: Increase Portion Size Gradually During the first six weeks of pregnancy, maintain your dog's regular feeding schedule but increase portion sizes by about 25%. During the last three weeks, further boost their intake by an additional 10-20%, depending on their weight gain and activity level. Guideline: Most female dogs need approximately 40-50% more food than usual during late pregnancy. However, avoid overfeeding as obesity can lead to complications during labor. Step 4: Provide Small, Frequent Meals Divide daily rations into smaller meals offered four times per day instead of two larger ones. Smaller quantities are easier to digest and help prevent bloat, a life-threatening condition common among large breeds. Step 5: Monitor Weight Gain Weigh your dog weekly to ensure healthy weight gain - ideally, she should gain around 15-20% of her pre-pregnancy body weight. If there are significant deviations from this range, consult your vet. Step 6: Supplement With Vitamins and Minerals Discuss supplementation with your vet. Calcium and phosphorus become critical near the end of pregnancy, particularly when natural milk production begins. Your vet might recommend calcium-rich foods or supplements starting two weeks prior to expected delivery. Step 7: Encourage Hydration Ensure fresh water is always available. Proper hydration supports fetal development, maintains optimal bodily functions, and facilitates smooth delivery. Step 8: Watch Out For Changes in Appetite or Behavior Loss of appetite, lethargy, restlessness, vomiting, or diarrhea close to the estimated due date warrants immediate veterinary attention. These signs could indicate impending labor or other health concerns requiring professional intervention. Conclusion: Feeding a pregnant dog shortly before labor involves careful planning, monitoring, and adjustment. By following these steps, you provide your pet with the necessary nutrients for a successful birth and postpartum recovery. Always remember that consulting with your trusted veterinarian remains vital throughout this journey. Aunt Kathy brought some drawing paper, didn't she? Okay sing here we are together XXX. Then, wash the bottle to remove all labels. Dry it with a towel. Turn the bottle over. Ask your child which tool you should use to cut or poke two small holes in the bottom of the bottle (scissors/knife). Throughout the activity, make sure your child is a safe distance away every time you cut the holes. When done, thread twine or wire through one hole and out the other. Loosely tie the twine or wire together to make a loop for hanging. The bottom of the bottle will become the top of the feeder. Cut or poke two holes on opposite sides of the bottle, three inches from the bottle cap. Make the holes just large enough for a chopstick to fit through. Ask your child which part should go through the holes (chopstick). Insert the chopstick; this will serve as a perch for the birds to sit on. Cut or poke one-third-inch holes two inches above the perch. This is where the birds will get the seed. Turn the bottle right-side up. Unscrew the cap and fill half the bottle with birdseed. Replace the cap. Turn the bottle over. Tie the twine or wire around a tree limb or hang outside a window. Watch the birds that come to your feeder. Title: How to Draw a Cartoon Koala Drawing can be a fun and rewarding hobby, and with practice, anyone can learn to draw well. This tutorial will guide you through the process of drawing a cartoon koala. Follow these steps carefully and don't forget to take your time! ### Step 1: Gather Your Materials Before starting, make sure you have all necessary materials ready. You will need: * A pencil (HB or 2B is ideal) * An eraser * Paper or a sketchbook * A ruler (optional) A printout of this tutorial might also be helpful for reference as you work. ### Step 2: Sketch Basic Shapes Begin by lightly sketching basic shapes that form the foundation of your koala. Start with two overlapping circles for the body - one slightly larger than the other. Then add a smaller circle above for the head and an oval beneath for the snout. Lastly, sketch two almond shapes for the ears. These guides help ensure accurate proportions and placement. ![Basic shapes](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Draw-a-Cartoon-Koala-Step-2-Version-3.jpg/aid456780-v4- Can you make a line that goes up and down? It is often very helpful to know what is going to happen to help prepare for these future events. Predictions are based on the idea that two beginning positions that are like each other will have similar results. By watching something happen, it is possible to predict what will happen if something similar happens. Predictions are given by science or fortune tellers or horoscopes. Title: Get Free Health Care for Your Children Through Their School Introduction: Health care can be expensive, especially for families with multiple children. However, many schools offer free health care services that you may not be aware of. This tutorial will guide you through the process of accessing these services for your children. By following these steps, you'll ensure your child's well-being without breaking the bank. Step 1: Understand the Services Offered Research what health care services are available at your child's school. These may include: * Vision and hearing screenings * Immunizations * Dental checkups * Mental health counseling * Nutrition advice Key Tip: Each school district has different programs, so make sure to check with your child's school directly or visit their website for specific information. Step 2: Check Eligibility Requirements Find out if there are any income requirements or other criteria you need to meet to qualify for these services. Some schools provide free healthcare to all students, while others have certain qualifications. Guideline: If your family meets federal poverty level guidelines, your child is more likely to be eligible for free health care services at school. Step 3: Gather Necessary Documents Prepare necessary documents such as proof of residency, income statements, or insurance cards before applying for the program. Having this paperwork ready saves time and makes the application process smoother. Step 4: Apply for the Program Submit an application form provided by the school or district office along with required documentation. Applications can usually be found online or obtained from the school administration office. Key Tip: Keep copies of everything you submit, including applications and correspondence related to them. Step 5: Follow Up After submitting your application, allow adequate processing time (this could range from days to weeks depending on the system). Contact the appropriate department if you haven't heard back within a reasonable period. Step 6: Schedule Appointments Once approved, schedule regular appointments for your child based on recommended schedules for each service. For example, annual physical examinations, semi-annual dental cleanings, etc. Step 7: Attend Parent-Teacher Conferences Use parent-teacher conferences as opportunities to discuss your child's overall health and wellness. Teachers often notice changes in behavior or appearance that might indicate underlying health issues. Step 8: Stay Involved Stay informed about ongoing health initiatives and campaigns at your child's school. Participate when possible and encourage your child to do the same. Step 9: Monitor Progress Keep track of your child's progress regarding their health goals. Celebrate achievements and address areas needing improvement together. Step 10: Advocate If you believe additional services should be offered or improved upon, communicate this respectfully with school administrators. You can also join school committees focused on health and wellness to contribute ideas and efforts towards enhancing these programs. Conclusion: By utilizing the free health care services provided by your child's school, you not only save money but also help maintain your child's optimal health. Regular check-ups and early detection of potential problems lead to better health outcomes. Plus, engaging with your child's school fosters stronger relationships between home and school - benefiting both academic success and social development. Title: How to Blend Eyeshadow like a Pro Introduction: Eyeshadow application is an art form that can completely transform your look. When done correctly, blended eyeshadow creates a smooth gradient effect that enhances the natural contours of your eye, making them appear larger, more open, and brighter. This wiki-style guide will take you through each step of the process with clear, easy-to-follow instructions so you too can master the technique of blending eyeshadow. What You'll Need: * Eye primer (optional but recommended) * A medium-toned eyeshadow shade close to your skin tone * A darker eyeshadow shade for definition * A lighter eyeshadow shade for highlighting * High quality makeup brushes - we recommend at least three types: flat shader brush, crease brush, and fluffy blending brush * Makeup remover or cotton swabs for any mistakes Step 1: Prep Your Eyes Before applying eyeshadow, prep your eyelids with either a dedicated eye primer or concealer to create a clean canvas and ensure longevity of your eyeshadow. Allow this layer to dry before moving onto the next step. Key Tip: Always start with less product than you think you need; you can always build up color as needed. Step 2: Apply Medium Base Color Using your flat shader brush, pick up some of the medium-toned eyeshadow and pat it all over your lid, focusing most heavily on the outer half of your lid. Be sure to blend well into the inner corner of your eye and slightly above your natural crease line to give the illusion of depth and dimension. Guideline: Avoid sweeping motions when initially laying down shadow colors, instead press gently to minimize fallout and maintain maximum pigmentation. Step 3: Define the Crease Next, grab your crease brush and select the darker eyeshadow hue. Gently tap off excess powder from the brush then place the tip of the brush just above where your eyeball meets your socket bone, pointing towards the outside edge of your brow. Sweep back and forth motion along the length of your crease to deepen its appearance without creating harsh lines. Key Tip: Keep your eyes open while doing this step to accurately gauge placement and intensity of the applied color. Step 4: Soften Transition Now it's time to soften the edges between the medium base color and the defined crease using a fluffy blending brush. Pick up leftover residual color from the darker eyeshadow or lightly dip into the medium base color if necessary. Use windshield wiper motions starting near your lash line working your way upward until there are no obvious transitions left. Step 5: Add Lightness To brighten the inner corners of your eyes and add lift to your brows, choose the lightest eyeshadow color available. With a smaller precision brush, dab the lightest color onto these areas, blending outwards toward the center of the lid. Step 6: Final Touches & Clean Up Check your work by looking straight ahead in good lighting. If any hard lines are visible, go back to Step 4 and continue blending until seamless. For further refinement, wet a small angled brush and carefully trace along your upper lash line as a makeshift eyeliner for added definition. Remember Practice Makes Perfect! Don't be discouraged if results aren't perfect immediately; practice regularly and experiment with different colors and techniques to find what works best for you. Happy blending! With its user-friendly interface and cross-platform compatibility, accepting contact requests has been made simple on iPhones and iPads. This wikiHow will guide you through the process with clear steps, helpful illustrations, and additional information to ensure your understanding. Just below you will see a drawing; the vector labeled c is being calculated according to your specifications for the vectors a and b. You move these by clicking on their tips and dragging them around the plane with the mouse. You can also "spin the plane" by clicking and dragging on other parts of the picture. In the drawing you see three vectors a, b, and c. The vector c is calculated from the vectors a and b using the vector cross product. In mathematical notation you write: c = a x b Magnetic forces are described using the vector cross product. So are the "torques" governing spinning and rotating objects. And there are still more examples. It is important to understand that any two vectors a and b lie in some plane. This is the plane indicated in the applet's output. The vector c calculated using the cross-product rules is always perpendicular (or "normal") to this plane. To help you understand these relationships, you can spin the plane shown in the figure. Place the mouse on some spot on the plane, click, and move the mouse around. The plane and the three vectors all rotate to accomodate the new perspective. We'll discuss how to figure out if c points "up" or "down" later on. Vectors have been denoted here using boldfaced characters. We denote the magnitude (or length) of the vector a as just "a" (without boldface). Note that a magnitude is always a positive number. The magnitude c of the cross product is given by the formula c = ab sin(\phi), where \phi is the angle included between a and b. The direction of c has only been defined as perpendicular to the plane of a and b. To determine whether c points up or down from the plane, you use a "right-hand" rule. The right hand rule is difficult to explain in words, but try the following exercise. Point the four fingers of your right hand so that they point in the same direction as the vector a. Now get your hand organized so that you can swing the palm of your hand from a to b. If you succeeded, then your thumb will point along c. See if you can make sense of this right-hand rule by checking it against the applet's output. It's called the Easter Wok leads the way. Get mixed up with no boy with no watch. Pluto, which was considered as a planet until last year, is at the farthest reaches of the Solar System. This small celestial body is a very "cold" place, with a nominal temperature of -238o C. Inside this freezing coldness, Pluto has a very thin atmosphere. However, the atmosphere is in a gaseous form only during those times when its elliptical orbit carries it close to the Sun. At other times, the atmosphere turns into an ice mass. In short, Pluto is a dead mass of ice. The diameter of Pluto is 2,300 kilometers. One year in Pluton, which is 6.0 billion kilometers away from the Sun, equals to 248 Earth years. Its size is 1/6 of our Moon. Its density is twice that of water. It is the orb with the most elliptical angle of orbit . Therefore, it was closer to the Sun than Neptune was between the years 1978 and 2000. Its only known satellite remained as Charon for a long time. Charon always shows the same face to Pluto, just like the Moon does to Earth. In 2005, it was found that Pluto had two more satellites, these satellites were named as Hydra and Nix in the year 2006. Rationale: Before children learn to read and write, they need to learn first, that letters of the alphabet stand for phonemes, and spelling words map out phonemes in spoken words. Of all of the phonemes, short vowels are the hardest to identify. This lesson will teach children to identify /a/ (short a), and how to recognize /a/ in spoken words. This teaches children a meaningful representation of a letter symbol, and then it will give them practice on finding /a/ in different words. Materials: primary paper and pencil; chart with pockets on it; class set of cards with a and ? on opposite sides; drawing paper and crayons; fishbowl; cut-out fish to go in bowls; fishing stick with magnet hanging from it; My Cap book; concentration game cards with short /a/ words on them; magazine pictures; worksheet. 1. Introduce lesson and explain to students that our written is like a secret code. But the tricky part is learning what sound stands for what letter. Today class, we are going to work on to spot the /a/ mouth move. At first, /a/ will be kind of hard to spot in works, but as we keep practicing this, it will get easier to /a/ in words. 2. Ask students: "Has anyone ever gone fishing? Well, at each group table, I have a fish bowl. In each fish bowl, are little fish and three sharks. But, on each fish are some words with the short /a/ sound and each person is to use your fishing pole and stick the magnet in the bowl to get a fish. If you pull out a shark, everyone puts all of his or her words back in the fish bowl, but you get to keep the shark out. Do this for the two remaining sharks, and the person with the most fish at the end of the game wins." 3. Have class get out their primary writing tablets and a pencil. Say to students, "Boys and girls, we can use the letter "a" to spell /a/. Let's try to write it. Start at the middle line, and draw a circle to the left hit the bottom line and circle back up to where you started. Then draw a straight line down to the bottom line. I am going to walk around and look at everyone's a. So remember, we are practicing the short /a/, and if you see 'a' in a word, this is the signal for the short /a/ sound." 4. Divide class into pairs. Demonstrate with one student, and teach class to play concentration. Have short /a/ words written down on each card. Turn cards face down and each student will take a turn and turn over two cards. If the words on the two cards rhyme, then you put those cards aside and go again. If the next two cards do not rhyme, the next person gets to take a turn. 5. Read My Cap to the class and discuss story. Read it again and have students raise their hands when they hear the /a/ sound in words. List these words on the board, and then have each child draw their favorite cap and write a message of why they like it, using inventive spelling. Each child will then show their work to the class. 6. For assessment, have entire class come back to middle of room, and call on one child at a time and ask the students if /a/ is in the words I say to them. "Do you hear /a/ in bark or beak? Walk or run? Banana or peach? Apple or fruit? Cup or glass? Tangerine or pineapple?" Now, pass out cards to every student. The cards have "a" on one side, and "?" on the other side. Tell the children to hold up the "a" side if they hear /a/ in the word given, or hold "?" if they do not hear the /a/ in the word. Give words one by one, such as ask, after, Auburn, hot, night, tomato, his, up, air, tap, desk. Reference: Shealey, De me idea on pocket chart and concentration game). Murray, Bruce A. (ed.) There is no real difference in the genetic processes underlying artificial and natural selection, and the concept of artificial selection was used by Charles Darwin as an illustration of the wider process of natural selection. There are practical differences. Experimental studies of artificial selection show that "the rate of evolution in selection experiments is at least two orders of magnitude (that is 100 times) greater than any rate seen in nature or the fossil record". Do you want to do them on the ground? She doesn't give us seconds anymore. [moves a paper over on the table] Two more variable types you can use are short and float. The short variable type is used to store smaller number, and its range is between minus 32,768 and plus 32,767. Instead of using int in our code on the previous pages, we could have used short instead. You should only use short if you're sure that the values that you want to hold don't go above 32, 767 or below -32,768. The double value we used can store really big numbers of the floating point variety. Instead of using double, float can be used. When storing a value in a float variable, you need the letter "f" at the end. Like this: float first_number, second_number, answer; first_number = 10.5f; second_number = 20.8f; So the letter "f" goes after the number but before the semicolon at the end of the line. To see the difference between float and double, see below. With the variables you've been using, you can use the following symbols to do calculations: + (the plus sign) - (the minus sign) * (the multiplication sign is the asterisk sign) / (the divide sign is the forward slash) Try this exercise: Delete the plus symbol that is used to add first_number and second_number. Replace it with the symbols above, first the minus sign, then the multiplication sign, and then the divide. The answer to the final one, the divide, should give you a really big number. The number you should get for divide is 0.5048076923076923. This is because you used the double variable type. However, change the double to float. Then add the letter "f" to the end of the numbers. So your code should look like this: When you run the above code, the answer is now 0.5048077. Java has taken the first 6 numbers after the point and then rounded up the rest. So the double variable type can hold more numbers than float. (Double is a 64 bit number and float is only 32 bit.)
